```
        private void CreateToolbar(Transform parent)
        {
            _toolbarContainer = CreatePanel(parent, "Toolbar", 0, TOOLBAR_HEIGHT);
            var rect = _toolbarContainer.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            rect.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);
            rect.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);
            rect.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);
            rect.anchoredPosition = new Vector2(0, -TITLE_BAR_HEIGHT);
            rect.sizeDelta = new Vector2(0, TOOLBAR_HEIGHT);
            
            // No background - buttons shade themselves, inherits from main dialog-bg
            
            // Use HorizontalLayoutGroup for button layout with flexible wrapping
            var hlg = _toolbarContainer.AddComponent<HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
            hlg.spacing = 5;
            hlg.padding = new RectOffset(8, 8, 4, 4);
            hlg.childAlignment = TextAnchor.MiddleLeft;
            hlg.childControlWidth = false;
            hlg.childControlHeight = true;
            hlg.childForceExpandWidth = false;
            hlg.childForceExpandHeight = false;
            hlg.childScaleWidth = false;
            hlg.childScaleHeight = false;
            
            // Content size fitter to wrap buttons
            var csf = _toolbarContainer.AddComponent<ContentSizeFitter>();
            csf.horizontalFit = ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.Unconstrained;
            csf.verticalFit = ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
            
            _toolbarButtons.Clear();
            
            // File buttons - simple text, no unicode
            var newFileBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "New", 45);
            newFileBtn.onClick.AddListener(OnNewFile);
            AddSimpleTooltip(newFileBtn.gameObject, "Create new file");
            
            var newFolderBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "Folder", 55);
            newFolderBtn.onClick.AddListener(OnNewFolder);
            AddSimpleTooltip(newFolderBtn.gameObject, "Create new folder");
            
            var renameBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "Rename", 60);
            renameBtn.onClick.AddListener(OnRename);
            AddSimpleTooltip(renameBtn.gameObject, "Rename selected");
            
            var deleteBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "Delete", 55);
            deleteBtn.onClick.AddListener(OnDelete);
            AddSimpleTooltip(deleteBtn.gameObject, "Delete selected");
            
            var saveBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "Save", 45);
            saveBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => { SaveCurrentFile(); SaveFileSystem(); });
            AddSimpleTooltip(saveBtn.gameObject, "Save (Ctrl+S)");
            
            // Separator with orange rounded corners
            CreateSeparator(_toolbarContainer.transform);
            
            // Formatting buttons
            var boldBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "B", 28);
            boldBtn.GetComponentInChildren<TextMeshProUGUI>().fontStyle = FontStyles.Bold;
            boldBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => InsertTag("<b>", "</b>"));
            AddSimpleTooltip(boldBtn.gameObject, "Bold");
            
            var italicBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "I", 28);
            italicBtn.GetComponentInChildren<TextMeshProUGUI>().fontStyle = FontStyles.Italic;
            italicBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => InsertTag("<i>", "</i>"));
            AddSimpleTooltip(italicBtn.gameObject, "Italic");
            
            var headerBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "H", 28);
            headerBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => InsertTag("<size=120%><b>", "</b></size>"));
            AddSimpleTooltip(headerBtn.gameObject, "Header");
            
            var colorBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "C", 28);
            colorBtn.GetComponentInChildren<TextMeshProUGUI>().color = ACCENT;
            colorBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => InsertTag("<color=#FBB03B>", "</color>"));
            AddSimpleTooltip(colorBtn.gameObject, "Orange color");
            
            var bulletBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_toolbarContainer.transform, "-", 28);
            bulletBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => InsertText("\n- "));
            AddSimpleTooltip(bulletBtn.gameObject, "Bullet point");
        }
```

```
        private void CreateToolbar(Transform parent)
        {
            _toolbarContainer = CreatePanel(parent, "Toolbar", 0, TOOLBAR_HEIGHT);
            var rect = _toolbarContainer.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            rect.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);
            rect.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);
            rect.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);
            rect.anchoredPosition = new Vector2(0, -TITLE_BAR_HEIGHT);
            rect.sizeDelta = new Vector2(0, TOOLBAR_HEIGHT);
            
            // No background - buttons shade themselves, inherits from main dialog-bg
            
            // Use HorizontalLayoutGroup for button layout
            var hlg = _toolbarContainer.AddComponent<HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
            hlg.spacing = 5;
            hlg.padding = new RectOffset(8, 8, 4, 4);
            hlg.childAlignment = TextAnchor.MiddleLeft;
            hlg.childControlWidth = false;
            hlg.childControlHeight = false;
            hlg.childForceExpandWidth = false;
            hlg.childForceExpandHeight = false;
            
            _toolbarButtons.Clear();
            
            // File buttons
```

```
        private void CreateToolbar(Transform parent)
        {
            _toolbarContainer = CreatePanel(parent, "Toolbar", 0, TOOLBAR_HEIGHT);
            var rect = _toolbarContainer.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            rect.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);
            rect.anchorMax = new Vector2(1, 1);
            rect.pivot = new Vector2(0.5f, 1);
            rect.anchoredPosition = new Vector2(0, -TITLE_BAR_HEIGHT);
            rect.sizeDelta = new Vector2(0, TOOLBAR_HEIGHT);
            
            // No background - buttons shade themselves, inherits from main dialog-bg
            
            // Use HorizontalLayoutGroup for button layout
            var hlg = _toolbarContainer.AddComponent<HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
            hlg.spacing = 5;
            hlg.padding = new RectOffset(8, 8, 4, 4);
            hlg.childAlignment = TextAnchor.MiddleLeft;
            hlg.childControlWidth = false;
            hlg.childControlHeight = true;
            hlg.childForceExpandWidth = false;
            hlg.childForceExpandHeight = false;
            
            _toolbarButtons.Clear();
            _fileButtons.Clear();
            _formatButtons.Clear();
            
            // === FILE BUTTONS GROUP ===
            _fileButtonsContainer = new GameObject("FileButtons");
            _fileButtonsContainer.transform.SetParent(_toolbarContainer.transform, false);
            var fileHlg = _fileButtonsContainer.AddComponent<HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
            fileHlg.spacing = 5;
            fileHlg.childControlWidth = false;
            fileHlg.childControlHeight = true;
            fileHlg.childForceExpandWidth = false;
            var fileLE = _fileButtonsContainer.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
            fileLE.flexibleWidth = 0;
            
            var newFileBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_fileButtonsContainer.transform, "New", 45);
            newFileBtn.onClick.AddListener(OnNewFile);
            _fileButtons.Add(newFileBtn.gameObject);
            
            var newFolderBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_fileButtonsContainer.transform, "Folder", 55);
            newFolderBtn.onClick.AddListener(OnNewFolder);

            var deleteBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_fileButtonsContainer.transform, "Delete", 55);

            var saveBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_fileButtonsContainer.transform, "Save", 45);

            sepImg.raycastTarget = false;
            
            // === FORMAT BUTTONS GROUP ===
            _formatButtonsContainer = new GameObject("FormatButtons");
            _formatButtonsContainer.transform.SetParent(_toolbarContainer.transform, false);
            var formatHlg = _formatButtonsContainer.AddComponent<HorizontalLayoutGroup>();
            formatHlg.spacing = 5;
            formatHlg.childControlWidth = false;
            formatHlg.childControlHeight = true;
            formatHlg.childForceExpandWidth = false;
            var formatLE = _formatButtonsContainer.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
            formatLE.flexibleWidth = 0;
            
            var boldBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_formatButtonsContainer.transform, "B", 28);
            boldBtn.GetComponentInChildren<TextMeshProUGUI>().fontStyle = FontStyles.Bold;
            boldBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => InsertTag("<b>", "</b>"));
            _formatButtons.Add(boldBtn.gameObject);
            
            var italicBtn = CreateToolbarBtn(_formatButtonsContainer.transform, "I", 28);
            italicBtn.GetComponentInChildren<TextMeshProUGUI>().fontStyle = FontStyles.Italic;
            italicBtn.onClick.AddListener(() => InsertTag("<i>", "</i>"));
```

```
        private void CreateDropdownMenus()
        {
            // File dropdown menu
            _fileDropdownMenu = CreateDropdownPanel(_windowPanel.transform, "FileDropdown");
            CreateDropdownItem(_fileDropdownMenu.transform, "New", OnNewFile);
            CreateDropdownItem(_fileDropdownMenu.transform, "Folder", OnNewFolder);
            CreateDropdownItem(_fileDropdownMenu.transform, "Rename", OnRename);
            CreateDropdownItem(_fileDropdownMenu.transform, "Delete", OnDelete);
            CreateDropdownItem(_fileDropdownMenu.transform, "Save", () => { SaveCurrentFile(); SaveFileSystem(); });
            _fileDropdownMenu.SetActive(false);
            
            // Format dropdown menu
            _formatDropdownMenu = CreateDropdownPanel(_windowPanel.transform, "FormatDropdown");
            CreateDropdownItem(_formatDropdownMenu.transform, "Bold", () => InsertTag("<b>", "</b>"));
            CreateDropdownItem(_formatDropdownMenu.transform, "Italic", () => InsertTag("<i>", "</i>"));
            CreateDropdownItem(_formatDropdownMenu.transform, "Header", () => InsertTag("<size=120%><b>", "</b></size>"));
            CreateDropdownItem(_formatDropdownMenu.transform, "Color", () => InsertTag("<color=#FBB03B>", "</color>"));
            CreateDropdownItem(_formatDropdownMenu.transform, "Bullet", () => InsertText("\n- "));
            _formatDropdownMenu.SetActive(false);
        }
        
        private GameObject CreateDropdownPanel(Transform parent, string name)
        {
            var panel = new GameObject(name);
            panel.transform.SetParent(parent, false);
            var rect = panel.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            rect.pivot = new Vector2(0, 1);
            rect.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 1);
            rect.anchorMax = new Vector2(0, 1);
            
            // Background with dialog-bg sprite
            var bg = panel.AddComponent<Image>();
            var bgSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("dialog-bg.png", 12);
            if (bgSprite != null) { bg.sprite = bgSprite; bg.type = Image.Type.Sliced; }
            bg.color = new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, 0.9f);
            
            // Outline
            var outlineGO = new GameObject("Outline");
            outlineGO.transform.SetParent(panel.transform, false);
            var outlineRect = outlineGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            outlineRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            outlineRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            outlineRect.offsetMin = Vector2.zero;
            outlineRect.offsetMax = Vector2.zero;
            var outlineImg = outlineGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            var outlineSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("dialog-outline.png", 12);
            if (outlineSprite != null) { outlineImg.sprite = outlineSprite; outlineImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced; }
            outlineImg.color = new Color(0.996f, 0.353f, 0.086f, 1f);
            outlineImg.raycastTarget = false;
            
            var vlg = panel.AddComponent<VerticalLayoutGroup>();
            vlg.spacing = 2;
            vlg.padding = new RectOffset(6, 6, 6, 6);
            vlg.childControlWidth = true;
            vlg.childControlHeight = false;
            vlg.childForceExpandWidth = true;
            
            var csf = panel.AddComponent<ContentSizeFitter>();
            csf.horizontalFit = ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
            csf.verticalFit = ContentSizeFitter.FitMode.PreferredSize;
            
            return panel;
        }
        
        private void CreateDropdownItem(Transform parent, string label, Action onClick)
        {
            var item = new GameObject(label);
            item.transform.SetParent(parent, false);
            
            var le = item.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
            le.preferredWidth = 80;
            le.preferredHeight = 24;
            
            var bg = item.AddComponent<Image>();
            bg.color = BTN_NORMAL;
            
            var btn = item.AddComponent<Button>();
            btn.targetGraphic = bg;
            var colors = btn.colors;
            colors.normalColor = BTN_NORMAL;
            colors.highlightedColor = BTN_HOVER;
            colors.pressedColor = BTN_PRESS;
            btn.colors = colors;
            btn.onClick.AddListener(() => { onClick?.Invoke(); HideAllDropdowns(); });
            
            var textGO = new GameObject("Text");
            textGO.transform.SetParent(item.transform, false);
            var text = textGO.AddComponent<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            text.text = label;
            text.fontSize = 12;
            text.color = TEXT_NORMAL;
            text.alignment = TextAlignmentOptions.MidlineLeft;
            var textRect = textGO.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            textRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            textRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            textRect.offsetMin = new Vector2(8, 0);
            textRect.offsetMax = new Vector2(-4, 0);
        }
        
        private void ToggleFileDropdown()
        {
            bool show = !_fileDropdownMenu.activeSelf;
            HideAllDropdowns();
            if (show)
            {
                _fileDropdownMenu.SetActive(true);
                PositionDropdown(_fileDropdownMenu, _fileDropdownBtn.gameObject);
            }
        }
        
        private void ToggleFormatDropdown()
        {
            bool show = !_formatDropdownMenu.activeSelf;
            HideAllDropdowns();
            if (show)
            {
                _formatDropdownMenu.SetActive(true);
                PositionDropdown(_formatDropdownMenu, _formatDropdownBtn.gameObject);
            }
        }
        
        private void PositionDropdown(GameObject dropdown, GameObject anchor)
        {
            var dropRect = dropdown.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            var anchorRect = anchor.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            
            // Position below the anchor button
            Vector3[] corners = new Vector3[4];
            anchorRect.GetWorldCorners(corners);
            dropRect.position = new Vector3(corners[0].x, corners[0].y, 0);
        }
        
        private void HideAllDropdowns()
        {
            if (_fileDropdownMenu != null) _fileDropdownMenu.SetActive(false);
            if (_formatDropdownMenu != null) _formatDropdownMenu.SetActive(false);
        }
        
        private void UpdateToolbarLayout()
        {
            if (_windowRect == null) return;
            
            float windowWidth = _windowRect.sizeDelta.x;
            bool shouldCollapse = windowWidth < COLLAPSE_THRESHOLD;
            
            if (shouldCollapse != _isCollapsed)
            {
                _isCollapsed = shouldCollapse;
                
                // Toggle between expanded and collapsed modes
                _fileButtonsContainer.SetActive(!shouldCollapse);
                _formatButtonsContainer.SetActive(!shouldCollapse);
                _separatorGO.SetActive(!shouldCollapse);
                
                _fileDropdownBtn.gameObject.SetActive(shouldCollapse);
                _formatDropdownBtn.gameObject.SetActive(shouldCollapse);
                
                HideAllDropdowns();
            }
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Creates the main content area containing file tree and editor
        /// </summary>
        private void CreateMainContent(Transform parent)
        {
            // Container for file tree and editor
            var contentArea = CreatePanel(parent, "ContentArea", 0, 0);
            var contentRect = contentArea.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            contentRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            contentRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            contentRect.offsetMin = new Vector2(5, 5);
```

```
        public static void UpdateWindow()
        {
            if (Instance == null || !IsOpen) return;
            
            // Track focus state like IC10 editor does
            if (Instance._editorInputField != null)
            {
                Instance._editorIsFocused = Instance._editorInputField.isFocused;
            }
            
            if (UnityEngine.Input.GetKey(KeyCode.LeftControl) && UnityEngine.Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.S))
            {
                Instance.SaveCurrentFile();
                Instance.SaveFileSystem();
            }
            
            if (UnityEngine.Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Escape))
            {
                Instance.Hide();
            }
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        public static void UpdateWindow()
        {
            if (Instance == null || !IsOpen) return;
            
            // Update responsive toolbar layout based on window size
            Instance.UpdateToolbarLayout();
            
            // Track focus state like IC10 editor does
            if (Instance._editorInputField != null)
            {
                Instance._editorIsFocused = Instance._editorInputField.isFocused;
            }
            
            // Close dropdowns when clicking outside
            if (UnityEngine.Input.GetMouseButtonDown(0))
            {
                // Simple check - close dropdowns on any click, they'll reopen if clicking the button
                Instance.HideAllDropdowns();
            }
            
            if (UnityEngine.Input.GetKey(KeyCode.LeftControl) && UnityEngine.Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.S))
            {
                Instance.SaveCurrentFile();
                Instance.SaveFileSystem();
            }
            
            if (UnityEngine.Input.GetKeyDown(KeyCode.Escape))
            {
                Instance.HideAllDropdowns();
                Instance.Hide();
            }
        }
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>

The key insight is that the game's UI is built with **Unity prefabs** - the buttons are defined in the Unity editor, not created at runtime via code. Let me check for IC10BatchUploader which uses IMGUI: 

Now I have comprehensive research. Let me compile my findings:

---

## Research Report: Dynamic UI Implementation for Station Planner

### Issue #1: Button Color Already Correct
The `BTN_NORMAL` is already `new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, 0.5f)` which equals `#00000080`. No change needed.

### Issue #2 & #5: Why Buttons Don't Scale - Root Cause Analysis

**The fundamental problem**: We're trying to create **dynamic responsive layouts at runtime** using Unity's layout system, which is designed for **static prefab-based UIs**.

#### How the IC10 Editor Actually Works:
1. **Buttons are Unity prefabs** - Created in Unity Editor with explicit sizes
2. **Layout is static** - The IC10 toolbar does NOT resize dynamically
3. **Window has fixed minimum size** - See `ClampWindowSize()` in `InputSourceCode.cs`:
```csharp
private void ClampWindowSize()
{
    float num = Mathf.Min((float)Screen.width, this._defaultWidth);
    if (num < 800f)
        num = 800f;  // Fixed minimum!
    this.Window.sizeDelta = new Vector2(num, this.Window.sizeDelta.y);
}
```

**The game DOES NOT have dynamic responsive toolbar buttons** - it has a fixed minimum window size.

#### Why Our HorizontalLayoutGroup Fails:

| Problem | Cause |
|---------|-------|
| Buttons too tall | `childControlHeight = true` makes all children fill parent height |
| Buttons overlapping | Nested LayoutGroups with conflicting settings |
| No responsive behavior | `flexibleWidth = 0` prevents shrinking, no overflow handling |

### Issue #3: Unity uGUI Layout System - First Principles

#### LayoutElement Priority (Unity Official):
```
1. Minimum sizes are allocated first
2. Preferred sizes if room available  
3. Flexible sizes for extra space distribution
```

**Key Insight**: The layout system is **allocation-based**, not **responsive**. It doesn't have:
- No built-in overflow menus
- No "collapse to dropdown" behavior
- No responsive breakpoints

#### Correct Pattern for Fixed Toolbar (like IC10):

```csharp
// Toolbar - FIXED SIZE, no layout groups
var toolbar = CreatePanel(parent, "Toolbar", windowWidth, 40);

// Buttons - positioned absolutely, not via layout
CreateButtonAt(toolbar, "New", x: 10, width: 60);
CreateButtonAt(toolbar, "Save", x: 75, width: 60);
// etc.
```

#### Pattern for Dynamic Toolbar (custom implementation needed):

```csharp
void Update() {
    float availableWidth = _toolbarRect.rect.width;
    float totalButtonWidth = CalculateTotalButtonWidth();
    
    if (totalButtonWidth > availableWidth) {
        // Hide buttons that don't fit, show "More" dropdown
        CollapseToolbar();
    } else {
        // Show all buttons
        ExpandToolbar();
    }
}
```

### Issue #4: IMGUI Feasibility Assessment

| Feature | IMGUI Support | Notes |
|---------|--------------|-------|
| Custom fonts | ⚠️ TrueType only | No TextMeshPro, lower quality |
| Rounded corners | ⚠️ Custom textures | Must create programmatically |
| 9-slice sprites | ✅ Yes | Via `GUIStyle.border` |
| Semi-transparent bg | ✅ Yes | Easy with `Color(r,g,b, alpha)` |
| Responsive layout | ✅ Built-in | `GUILayout.FlexibleSpace()` |
| Font matching | ❌ No | Cannot use game's TMP fonts |

**IMGUI Advantages:**
- Simpler layout system (`GUILayout.BeginHorizontal()`)
- Built-in flexible spacing
- No prefabs needed
- Works well with BepInEx mods

**IMGUI Disadvantages:**
- Cannot match game's TextMeshPro fonts exactly
- Lower quality text rendering (bitmap vs SDF)
- Buttons would look different from game UI
- Redraws every frame (minor perf impact)

**Verdict**: IMGUI is viable for **tool windows** but **NOT recommended** for matching game aesthetic.

### Recommendations

#### Option A: Match IC10 Exactly (Fixed Size)
Set a minimum window width and don't try to make buttons responsive:

```csharp
private const float MIN_WINDOW_WIDTH = 600f; // Enough for all buttons

private void ClampWindowSize()
{
    float width = Mathf.Max(MIN_WINDOW_WIDTH, _windowRect.sizeDelta.x);
    _windowRect.sizeDelta = new Vector2(width, _windowRect.sizeDelta.y);
}
```

#### Option B: Custom Overflow System
Implement manual responsive behavior:

```csharp
// Track button GameObjects
private List<GameObject> _allButtons = new List<GameObject>();
private GameObject _moreButton;
private GameObject _dropdownPanel;

void UpdateLayout() {
    float available = _toolbarRect.rect.width - 100; // Reserve space for "More"
    float used = 0;
    
    foreach (var btn in _allButtons) {
        float btnWidth = btn.GetComponent<LayoutElement>().preferredWidth;
        if (used + btnWidth < available) {
            btn.SetActive(true);
            used += btnWidth + 5; // +spacing
        } else {
            btn.SetActive(false);
            // Add to dropdown instead
        }
    }
    
    _moreButton.SetActive(_allButtons.Any(b => !b.activeSelf));
}
```

#### Option C: Different UI Approach
Use a **scrollable toolbar** instead of collapsing:

```csharp
// Make toolbar a horizontal scroll view
var scrollRect = toolbar.AddComponent<ScrollRect>();
scrollRect.horizontal = true;
scrollRect.vertical = false;
// Users scroll to see more buttons
```

```
        private Button CreateToolbarBtn(Transform parent, string label, float width)
        {
            var go = new GameObject(label);
            go.transform.SetParent(parent, false);
            
            // Set explicit size via RectTransform - this is key!
            var rectTransform = go.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            rectTransform.sizeDelta = new Vector2(width, 26);
            
            // LayoutElement tells the layout group our preferred size
            var le = go.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
            le.preferredWidth = width;
            le.preferredHeight = 26;
            le.minWidth = width;      // Don't allow shrinking
            le.minHeight = 26;
            le.flexibleWidth = 0;     // Don't expand
            le.flexibleHeight = 0;
            
            // Use IC10-style button-bg sprite with black 50% color
            var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
            var buttonSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("button-bg.png", 4);
            if (buttonSprite != null)
            {
                img.sprite = buttonSprite;
                img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            img.color = BTN_NORMAL; // #00000080
            
            var btn = go.AddComponent<Button>();
            btn.targetGraphic = img;
            var colors = btn.colors;
            colors.normalColor = Color.white;  // Use white so Image.color shows through
            colors.highlightedColor = new Color(1f, 1f, 1f, 1.3f);  // Brighten on hover
            colors.pressedColor = new Color(0.7f, 0.7f, 0.7f, 1f);  // Darken on press
            colors.selectedColor = Color.white;
            colors.colorMultiplier = 1f;
            colors.fadeDuration = 0.1f;
            btn.colors = colors;
            
            var textGO = new GameObject("Text");
            textGO.transform.SetParent(go.transform, false);
            var text = textGO.AddComponent<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            text.text = label;
            text.fontSize = 12;
            text.color = TEXT_NORMAL;
            text.alignment = TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
            text.raycastTarget = false;
            
            var textRect = textGO.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            textRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            textRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            textRect.offsetMin = new Vector2(2, 0);
            textRect.offsetMax = new Vector2(-2, 0);
            
            _toolbarButtons.Add(btn);
            return btn;
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        private Button CreateToolbarBtn(Transform parent, string label, float width)
        {
            var go = new GameObject(label);
            go.transform.SetParent(parent, false);
            go.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            
            var le = go.AddComponent<LayoutElement>();
            le.preferredWidth = width;
            le.preferredHeight = 26;
            le.minWidth = 26; // Allow shrinking but keep minimum
            le.flexibleWidth = 0;
            
            // Use IC10-style button-bg sprite with darker blue color
            var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
            var buttonSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("button-bg.png", 4);
            if (buttonSprite != null)
            {
                img.sprite = buttonSprite;
                img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            img.color = BTN_NORMAL; // Darker blue matching IC10
            
            var btn = go.AddComponent<Button>();
            btn.targetGraphic = img;
            var colors = btn.colors;
            colors.normalColor = BTN_NORMAL;
            colors.highlightedColor = BTN_HOVER;
            colors.pressedColor = BTN_PRESS;
            colors.selectedColor = BTN_NORMAL;
            colors.colorMultiplier = 1f;
            colors.fadeDuration = 0.1f;
            btn.colors = colors;
            
            var textGO = new GameObject("Text");
            textGO.transform.SetParent(go.transform, false);
            var text = textGO.AddComponent<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            text.text = label;
            text.fontSize = 12;
            text.color = TEXT_NORMAL;
            text.alignment = TextAlignmentOptions.Center;
            text.raycastTarget = false;
            
            var textRect = textGO.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            textRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            textRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            textRect.offsetMin = new Vector2(2, 0);
            textRect.offsetMax = new Vector2(-2, 0);
            
            _toolbarButtons.Add(btn);
            return btn;
        }
```

```
            // Use IC10-style button-bg sprite
            var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
            var buttonSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("button-bg.png", 4);
            if (buttonSprite != null)
            {
                img.sprite = buttonSprite;
                img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            img.color = Color.white; // White base, button colors control the actual color
            
            var btn = go.AddComponent<Button>();
            btn.targetGraphic = img;
            var colors = btn.colors;
            // IC10 button colors:
            // Normal: #00000080 (black 50%)
            // Hover: #FFFFFF80 (white 50%) 
            // Press: #FF651620 (red ~12%)
            colors.normalColor = BTN_NORMAL;                                    // #00000080
            colors.highlightedColor = new Color(1f, 1f, 1f, 0.5f);              // #FFFFFF80
            colors.pressedColor = new Color(1f, 0.396f, 0.125f, 0.125f);        // #FF651620
            colors.selectedColor = BTN_NORMAL;
            colors.colorMultiplier = 1f;
            colors.fadeDuration = 0.1f;
            btn.colors = colors;
```

```
            colors.pressedColor = new Color(0.7f, 0.7f, 0.7f, 1f);  // Darken on press
```

- newString:

```
        private void AppendGameObjectInfo(StringBuilder sb, GameObject go, int index)
        {
            sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700>[{index}] {go.name}</color>");
            
            // Get hierarchy path (up to 5 levels for more context)
            string path = GetHierarchyPath(go.transform, 5);
            sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#A0A0A0>Path: {path}</color>");
            
            // RectTransform details
            var rect = go.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            if (rect != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#C080C0>RectTransform:</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>sizeDelta: {rect.sizeDelta}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>anchorMin: {rect.anchorMin} anchorMax: {rect.anchorMax}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>pivot: {rect.pivot}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>offsetMin: {rect.offsetMin} offsetMax: {rect.offsetMax}</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for Image component and sprite
            var image = go.GetComponent<Image>();
            if (image != null)
            {
                sb.Append("  <color=#80FF80>Image:</color> ");
                if (image.sprite != null)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFFFFF>{image.sprite.name}</color>");
                    if (image.sprite.texture != null)
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Texture: {image.sprite.texture.name}</color>");
                    
                    // Show sprite border for 9-slice info
                    var border = image.sprite.border;
                    if (border != Vector4.zero)
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Border(9-slice): L={border.x} B={border.y} R={border.z} T={border.w}</color>");
                    
                    // Show sprite rect
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>SpriteRect: {image.sprite.rect}</color>");
                }
                else
                {
                    sb.AppendLine("<color=#808080>(no sprite)</color>");
                }
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Color: {ColorToHex(image.color)}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Type: {image.type}, RaycastTarget: {image.raycastTarget}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Material: {(image.material != null ? image.material.name : "null")}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>maskable: {image.maskable}, preserveAspect: {image.preserveAspect}</color>");
                
                // Check if it's a TranslucentImage
                var translucentImage = go.GetComponent<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImage>();
                if (translucentImage != null)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#FF80FF>TranslucentImage: spriteBlending={translucentImage.spriteBlending:F2}</color>");
                }
            }
            
            // Check ALL children for additional layers
            int childCount = go.transform.childCount;
            if (childCount > 0)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFAA00>Children ({childCount}):</color>");
                for (int i = 0; i < Mathf.Min(childCount, 8); i++)
                {
                    var child = go.transform.GetChild(i);
                    var childImg = child.GetComponent<Image>();
                    var childRect = child.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
                    
                    sb.Append($"    <color=#D0D000>{child.name}</color>");
                    if (childImg != null)
                    {
                        string spriteName = childImg.sprite != null ? childImg.sprite.name : "(no sprite)";
                        sb.Append($" → <color=#80FF80>{spriteName}</color>");
                        sb.Append($" color:{ColorToHex(childImg.color)}");
                        sb.Append($" type:{childImg.type}");
                    }
                    if (childRect != null)
                    {
                        sb.Append($" size:{childRect.sizeDelta}");
                    }
                    sb.AppendLine();
                }
                if (childCount > 8)
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>... and {childCount - 8} more children</color>");
            }
            
            // Check parent for sprites (to understand context)
            if (go.transform.parent != null)
            {
                var parentGO = go.transform.parent.gameObject;
                var parentImage = parentGO.GetComponent<Image>();
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#60A060>Parent: {parentGO.name}</color>");
                if (parentImage != null)
                {
                    string pSpriteName = parentImage.sprite != null ? parentImage.sprite.name : "(no sprite)";
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#60A060>Parent Image: {pSpriteName} color:{ColorToHex(parentImage.color)}</color>");
                }
                
                // Grandparent too for more context
                if (go.transform.parent.parent != null)
                {
                    var grandparent = go.transform.parent.parent.gameObject;
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#508050>Grandparent: {grandparent.name}</color>");
                    var gpImage = grandparent.GetComponent<Image>();
                    if (gpImage != null && gpImage.sprite != null)
                    {
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#508050>GP Image: {gpImage.sprite.name} color:{ColorToHex(gpImage.color)}</color>");
                    }
                }
            }
            
            // Check for siblings with sprites
            if (go.transform.parent != null)
            {
                int sibIdx = go.transform.GetSiblingIndex();
                int siblingCount = go.transform.parent.childCount;
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#6060A0>Sibling index: {sibIdx}/{siblingCount}</color>");
                
                // Show ALL siblings for context
                for (int i = 0; i < Mathf.Min(siblingCount, 6); i++)
                {
                    if (i == sibIdx) continue; // Skip self
                    var sibling = go.transform.parent.GetChild(i);
                    var sibImage = sibling.GetComponent<Image>();
                    if (sibImage != null)
                    {
                        string sSpr = sibImage.sprite != null ? sibImage.sprite.name : "(no sprite)";
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#6060A0>Sib[{i}] {sibling.name}: {sSpr} {ColorToHex(sibImage.color)}</color>");
                    }
                }
            }
            
            // Check for RawImage
            var rawImage = go.GetComponent<RawImage>();
            if (rawImage != null)
            {
                sb.Append("  <color=#80FF80>RawImage:</color> ");
                if (rawImage.texture != null)
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFFFFF>{rawImage.texture.name}</color>");
                else
                    sb.AppendLine("<color=#808080>(no texture)</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for TextMeshProUGUI
            var tmp = go.GetComponent<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            if (tmp != null)
            {
                string textPreview = tmp.text;
                if (textPreview.Length > 30)
                    textPreview = textPreview.Substring(0, 30) + "...";
                textPreview = textPreview.Replace("\n", "\\n").Replace("<", "〈").Replace(">", "〉");
                
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#80FFFF>TMP:</color> \"{textPreview}\"");
                if (tmp.font != null)
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Font: {tmp.font.name}</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for Button
            var button = go.GetComponent<Button>();
            if (button != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFFF80>Button</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for UiComponentRenderer
            var uiRenderer = go.GetComponent<UiComponentRenderer>();
            if (uiRenderer != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FF8080>UiComponentRenderer</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for Canvas
            var canvas = go.GetComponent<Canvas>();
            if (canvas != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#8080FF>Canvas: sortOrder={canvas.sortingOrder}, renderMode={canvas.renderMode}</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for CanvasGroup
            var canvasGroup = go.GetComponent<CanvasGroup>();
            if (canvasGroup != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#8080FF>CanvasGroup: alpha={canvasGroup.alpha}, blocksRaycasts={canvasGroup.blocksRaycasts}</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for Mask
            var mask = go.GetComponent<Mask>();
            if (mask != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FF80FF>Mask: showMaskGraphic={mask.showMaskGraphic}</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for RectMask2D  
            var rectMask = go.GetComponent<RectMask2D>();
            if (rectMask != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FF80FF>RectMask2D present</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for Shadow/Outline effects
            var shadow = go.GetComponent<Shadow>();
            if (shadow != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFA0A0>Shadow: color={ColorToHex(shadow.effectColor)}, dist={shadow.effectDistance}</color>");
            }
            
            var outline = go.GetComponent<Outline>();
            if (outline != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFA0A0>Outline: color={ColorToHex(outline.effectColor)}, dist={outline.effectDistance}</color>");
            }
            
            // List other notable components
            var components = go.GetComponents<Component>();
            foreach (var comp in components)
            {
                if (comp == null) continue;
                string typeName = comp.GetType().Name;
                
                // Skip common/already listed components
                if (typeName == "RectTransform" || typeName == "CanvasRenderer" || 
                    typeName == "Image" || typeName == "RawImage" || typeName == "Button" ||
                    typeName == "TextMeshProUGUI" || typeName == "Canvas" || 
                    typeName == "UiComponentRenderer" || typeName == "TranslucentImage" ||
                    typeName == "CanvasGroup" || typeName == "Mask" || typeName == "RectMask2D" ||
                    typeName == "Shadow" || typeName == "Outline")
                    continue;
                
                // Show interesting components
                if (typeName.Contains("Scroll") || typeName.Contains("Layout") || 
                    typeName.Contains("Mask") || typeName.Contains("Input") ||
                    typeName.Contains("Toggle") || typeName.Contains("Slider") ||
                    typeName.Contains("Dropdown") || typeName.Contains("Editor") ||
                    typeName.Contains("Panel") || typeName.Contains("Window") ||
                    typeName.Contains("Fitter") || typeName.Contains("Anim"))
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#C0C0C0>{typeName}</color>");
                }
            }
            
            sb.AppendLine();
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        private void AppendGameObjectInfo(StringBuilder sb, GameObject go, int index)
        {
            sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700>[{index}] {go.name}</color>");
            
            // Get hierarchy path (up to 3 levels)
            string path = GetHierarchyPath(go.transform, 3);
            sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#A0A0A0>Path: {path}</color>");
            
            // Check for Image component and sprite
            var image = go.GetComponent<Image>();
            if (image != null)
            {
                sb.Append("  <color=#80FF80>Image:</color> ");
                if (image.sprite != null)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFFFFF>{image.sprite.name}</color>");
                    if (image.sprite.texture != null)
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Texture: {image.sprite.texture.name}</color>");
                    
                    // Show sprite border for 9-slice info
                    var border = image.sprite.border;
                    if (border != Vector4.zero)
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Border(9-slice): L={border.x} B={border.y} R={border.z} T={border.w}</color>");
                }
                else
                {
                    sb.AppendLine("<color=#808080>(no sprite)</color>");
                }
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Color: {ColorToHex(image.color)}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Type: {image.type}, RaycastTarget: {image.raycastTarget}</color>");
                
                // Check if it's a TranslucentImage
                var translucentImage = go.GetComponent<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImage>();
                if (translucentImage != null)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#FF80FF>TranslucentImage: spriteBlending={translucentImage.spriteBlending:F2}</color>");
                }
            }
            
            // Check parent for sprites (to understand context)
            if (go.transform.parent != null)
            {
                var parentImage = go.transform.parent.GetComponent<Image>();
                if (parentImage != null && parentImage.sprite != null)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#60A060>Parent Image: {parentImage.sprite.name}</color>");
                }
            }
            
            // Check for siblings with sprites
            if (go.transform.parent != null)
            {
                int sibIdx = go.transform.GetSiblingIndex();
                if (sibIdx > 0)
                {
                    var prevSibling = go.transform.parent.GetChild(sibIdx - 1);
                    var prevImage = prevSibling.GetComponent<Image>();
                    if (prevImage != null && prevImage.sprite != null)
                    {
                        sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#6060A0>Prev Sibling: {prevSibling.name} → {prevImage.sprite.name}</color>");
                    }
                }
            }
            
            // Check for RawImage
            var rawImage = go.GetComponent<RawImage>();
            if (rawImage != null)
            {
                sb.Append("  <color=#80FF80>RawImage:</color> ");
                if (rawImage.texture != null)
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFFFFF>{rawImage.texture.name}</color>");
                else
                    sb.AppendLine("<color=#808080>(no texture)</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for TextMeshProUGUI
            var tmp = go.GetComponent<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            if (tmp != null)
            {
                string textPreview = tmp.text;
                if (textPreview.Length > 30)
                    textPreview = textPreview.Substring(0, 30) + "...";
                textPreview = textPreview.Replace("\n", "\\n").Replace("<", "〈").Replace(">", "〉");
                
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#80FFFF>TMP:</color> \"{textPreview}\"");
                if (tmp.font != null)
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Font: {tmp.font.name}</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for Button
            var button = go.GetComponent<Button>();
            if (button != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFFF80>Button</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for UiComponentRenderer
            var uiRenderer = go.GetComponent<UiComponentRenderer>();
            if (uiRenderer != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FF8080>UiComponentRenderer</color>");
            }
            
            // Check for Canvas
            var canvas = go.GetComponent<Canvas>();
            if (canvas != null)
            {
                sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#8080FF>Canvas: sortOrder={canvas.sortingOrder}</color>");
            }
            
            // List other notable components
            var components = go.GetComponents<Component>();
            foreach (var comp in components)
            {
                if (comp == null) continue;
                string typeName = comp.GetType().Name;
                
                // Skip common/already listed components
                if (typeName == "RectTransform" || typeName == "CanvasRenderer" || 
                    typeName == "Image" || typeName == "RawImage" || typeName == "Button" ||
                    typeName == "TextMeshProUGUI" || typeName == "Canvas" || 
                    typeName == "UiComponentRenderer" || typeName == "TranslucentImage")
                    continue;
                
                // Show interesting components
                if (typeName.Contains("Scroll") || typeName.Contains("Layout") || 
                    typeName.Contains("Mask") || typeName.Contains("Input") ||
                    typeName.Contains("Toggle") || typeName.Contains("Slider") ||
                    typeName.Contains("Dropdown") || typeName.Contains("Editor"))
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#C0C0C0>{typeName}</color>");
                }
            }
            
            sb.AppendLine();
        }
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user has two requests:

1. Fix button hover/press colors:
   - Hover: #FFFFFF80 (white at 50% opacity) - currently gets darker, should get lighter
   - Press: #FF651620 (red at ~12.5% opacity)

2. Investigate the mystery of how IC10 editor draws its input panel - there's a weird shading next to the outline in our editor that the IC10 editor doesn't have. The user wants me to analyze screenshots and figure out the source. They mention the parent object is "window shadow" which is a clue.

Let me first fix the button colors, then investigate the panel shading issue.

First, let me read the current state of the file to see the button code:

---

_**User (2026-01-05 00:02:12Z)**_

Here is a screenshot of what it shows when I hover over that darker area and then the screenshots of the two editors and how they are different. I want us to be rendering this stuff the same exact way the game does. The game code doesn't inform you how this is being rendered?!
First image is the tooltip second image is the game's verison and third image is our version


---

_**Agent (copilot/claude-sonnet-4.5)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**EXCELLENT!** Now I can see the exact problem. The IC10 editor's structure is completely different from ours:

**IC10's PanelInputCode structure:**
```
PanelInputCode (NO SPRITE - just flat color #00000DBF)
├── Image (window-shadow sprite, white #FFFFFE80)
│   ├── Image (dialog-bg sprite, #002A47FF with TranslucentImage)
│   └── Outline (dialog-outline sprite, ORANGE #FF6516FF)
└── [other content]
```

**Our current wrong structure:**

The shading issue stems from using a dialog-bg sprite with black tint for the inner dark area, whereas IC10 actually uses no sprite for PanelInputCode - just a flat color. The window-shadow and dialog-bg are designed as separate child layers.

```
        private void CreateFileTreePanel(Transform parent)
        {
            var panel = CreatePanel(parent, "FileTree", FILE_TREE_WIDTH, 0);
            var rect = panel.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            rect.anchorMin = new Vector2(0, 0);
            rect.anchorMax = new Vector2(0, 1);
            rect.pivot = new Vector2(0, 0.5f);
            rect.anchoredPosition = Vector2.zero;
            rect.sizeDelta = new Vector2(FILE_TREE_WIDTH, 0);
            
            // Root panel: NO SPRITE - just flat color like IC10's PanelInputCode
            var bg = panel.AddComponent<Image>();
            bg.sprite = null; // No sprite!
            bg.color = new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, 0.863f); // #00000DBF - almost transparent black like IC10
            bg.raycastTarget = true;
            
            // Layer 1: window-shadow (white at 50%)
            var shadowGO = new GameObject("Image");
            shadowGO.transform.SetParent(panel.transform, false);
            var shadowRect = shadowGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            shadowRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            shadowRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            shadowRect.offsetMin = Vector2.zero;
            shadowRect.offsetMax = Vector2.zero;
            var shadowImg = shadowGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            var shadowSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("window-shadow.png", 42);
            if (shadowSprite != null)
            {
                shadowImg.sprite = shadowSprite;
                shadowImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            shadowImg.color = new Color(1f, 1f, 1f, 0.5f); // #FFFFFE80 - white at 50%
            shadowImg.raycastTarget = true;
            
            // Layer 2: dialog-bg with TranslucentImage (child of shadow)
            var bgGO = new GameObject("Image");
            bgGO.transform.SetParent(shadowGO.transform, false);
            var bgRect = bgGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            bgRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            bgRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            bgRect.offsetMin = Vector2.zero;
            bgRect.offsetMax = Vector2.zero;
            var bgImg = bgGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            var bgSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("dialog-bg.png", 18);
            if (bgSprite != null)
            {
                bgImg.sprite = bgSprite;
                bgImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            bgImg.color = new Color(0f, 0.165f, 0.278f, 1f); // #002A47FF - full opacity dark blue
            bgImg.raycastTarget = true;
            
            // Add TranslucentImage component for blur effect like IC10
            var translucentImg = bgGO.AddComponent<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImage>();
            translucentImg.spriteBlending = 1.0f;
            
            // Layer 3: Dialog outline (ORANGE like IC10, child of shadow)
            var outlineGO = new GameObject("Outline");
            outlineGO.transform.SetParent(shadowGO.transform, false);
            var outlineRect = outlineGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            outlineRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            outlineRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            outlineRect.offsetMin = Vector2.zero;
            outlineRect.offsetMax = Vector2.zero;
            var outlineImg = outlineGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            var outlineSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("dialog-outline.png", 18);
            if (outlineSprite != null)

                outlineImg.sprite = outlineSprite;
                outlineImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            outlineImg.color = new Color(1f, 0.396f, 0.086f, 1f); // #FF6516FF - ORANGE like IC10
            outlineImg.raycastTarget = true;
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Excellent questions! Let me address each one and do some investigation.

**Texture vs Sprite in Unity:**
- **Texture2D** = raw pixel data (the PNG image)
- **Sprite** = a wrapper with metadata: 9-slice borders, pivot point, rect (which part of texture to use), etc.
- In asset bundles, both exist - the sprite references the texture and adds that metadata
- **Our code** loads PNG → creates Texture2D → manually creates Sprite with `Sprite.Create()` and our own border values

The game's sprites have their 9-slice borders baked in from the Unity editor. We're guessing the border size (12px).

**Why we can't find the IC10 creation code:**
The IC10 UI is defined in a **Unity Prefab file** (binary asset), NOT in C# code. The `InputSourceCode` class has:
```csharp
public TranslucentImage Background;  // Assigned in Unity Editor
public RectTransform LineParent;     // Assigned in Unity Editor
```
These are **serialized references** - drag-and-drop in Unity Editor. The hierarchy is pre-built in the prefab.

Let me check our code to understand why the outer outline looks correct but inner doesn't: 

I see! The **outer window** uses:
- dialog-bg with **blue color** `#002A47FF` (full opacity)
- dialog-outline with **orange color** `#FF5A16FF` (full opacity)
- border size **18** for outline, **12** for bg

But the **inner panels** use:
- dialog-bg with **BLACK color at 75%** `#000000BF`
- dialog-outline with **BLACK color at 80%** `#000000CC`
- border size **12** for both

```
            var image = go.GetComponent<Image>();
            if (image != null)
            {
                sb.Append("  <color=#80FF80>Image:</color> ");
                if (image.sprite != null)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFFFFF>{image.sprite.name}</color>");
                    if (image.sprite.texture != null)
                    {
                        var tex = image.sprite.texture;
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Texture: {tex.name} ({tex.width}x{tex.height})</color>");
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>TexFormat: {tex.format}, isReadable: {tex.isReadable}</color>");
                        
                        // Try to sample pixels if readable
                        if (tex.isReadable)
                        {
                            try
                            {
                                var centerPixel = tex.GetPixel(tex.width / 2, tex.height / 2);
                                var cornerPixel = tex.GetPixel(2, 2);
                                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#AAFFAA>CenterPixel: {ColorToHex(centerPixel)}</color>");
                                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#AAFFAA>CornerPixel: {ColorToHex(cornerPixel)}</color>");
                            }
                            catch { }
                        }
                    }
                    
                    // Show sprite border for 9-slice info
                    var border = image.sprite.border;
                    if (border != Vector4.zero)
                        sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Border(9-slice): L={border.x} B={border.y} R={border.z} T={border.w}</color>");
                    
                    // Show sprite rect
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>SpriteRect: {image.sprite.rect}</color>");
                }
                else
                {
                    sb.AppendLine("<color=#808080>(no sprite)</color>");
                }
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Color: {ColorToHex(image.color)}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Type: {image.type}, RaycastTarget: {image.raycastTarget}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>Material: {(image.material != null ? image.material.name : "null")}</color>");
                sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#808080>maskable: {image.maskable}, preserveAspect: {image.preserveAspect}</color>");
                
                // Check if it's a TranslucentImage
                var translucentImage = go.GetComponent<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImage>();
                if (translucentImage != null)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#FF80FF>TranslucentImage: spriteBlending={translucentImage.spriteBlending:F2}</color>");
                }
            }
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is asking several important questions:

1. **Texture vs Sprite difference** - What's the difference between a "texture" and a "sprite" in Unity asset bundles?

2. **Are we using texture or sprite?** - Looking at our code, we load PNG files and create Sprite objects from them using `Sprite.Create()`.

3. **Why outer outline doesn't have shading issues but inner does?** - This is a great observation! The main window's dialog-outline doesn't have the shading issue but the inner PanelInputCode area does.

4. **Why can't we find the IC10 panel creation code?** - The user is right - the hierarchy has to be created somewhere. The issue is that Unity prefabs are serialized binary assets, not code. The hierarchy is defined in a .prefab file in Unity editor, not in C# code.

Let me address these:

1. **Texture vs Sprite**: In Unity:
   - A **Texture2D** is just raw pixel data (the image itself)
   - A **Sprite** is a wrapper around a texture that adds metadata like: rect (which part of texture to use), pivot point, 9-slice borders, etc.
   - When you export from asset bundles, the "texture" is the raw PNG data, the "sprite" is the same image but with Unity's sprite metadata (borders, pivot, etc.)
   - Our code loads a PNG (texture) and creates a Sprite manually with `Sprite.Create()`, specifying our own border values

2. **Why we can't find the IC10 creation code**: The IC10 editor UI is defined in a **Unity Prefab** (.prefab file), not in C# code. The `InputSourceCode` class has serialized references like `public TranslucentImage Background;` - these are assigned in the Unity Editor by dragging objects onto the inspector, not created in code. The prefab contains the complete hierarchy with all the Image components, sprites, colors, etc. pre-configured.
   
I'm investigating the visual differences between the main window and inner panels. The key observation is the layering and component structure. The main window uses a window-shadow base layer, while our inner panels lack this critical component. Additionally, the main window's dialog background uses a TranslucentImage, which we're not implementing in the inner panels. This structural variation could be causing the shading discrepancy we're seeing.

The color choices are intriguing - the outer dialog uses an accent (orange) outline, whereas the inner panels use black. However, the color difference might not be the primary issue. The layering and image composition seem more significant in creating the visual effect.

I notice a critical structural difference between the outer and inner dialog rendering. The outer dialog uses three distinct layers - window-shadow, dialog-background, and dialog-outline - creating a proper visual hierarchy. In contrast, the inner panel lacks the window-shadow layer, potentially exposing underlying sprite artifacts.

The sprite's internal gradient might be revealing itself through the black 75% tint, causing unexpected lighter areas. This suggests the problem isn't just color selection, but how the sprite's inherent shading interacts with our tinting approach.

The investigation points toward understanding the dialog-bg.png sprite's internal composition and how its embedded highlights interact with our current rendering technique.

Digging deeper, I realize the UI is fundamentally defined through a Unity prefab - a binary asset encoding the entire visual hierarchy. The C# code merely references pre-configured objects, which explains why traditional code tracing won't reveal the element's creation mechanism.

Our manual sprite generation might be missing critical metadata present in the game's original sprite assets, potentially causing these subtle rendering discrepancies.

```
            // IC10's PanelInputCode uses NO SPRITE on the root - just a flat color!
            // (The rounded corners come from child layers in IC10, but we use flat color for simplicity)
            var bg = panel.AddComponent<Image>();
            bg.sprite = null; // No sprite - flat color only (matches IC10's PanelInputCode exactly)
            bg.color = new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, 0.75f); // #000000BF - matches IC10's PanelInputCode
            bg.raycastTarget = true;
            
            // Orange outline for file tree (matches IC10's orange visual theme)
            var outlineGO = new GameObject("Outline");
            outlineGO.transform.SetParent(panel.transform, false);
            var outlineRect = outlineGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            outlineRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            outlineRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            outlineRect.offsetMin = Vector2.zero;
            outlineRect.offsetMax = Vector2.zero;
            var outlineImg = outlineGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            var outlineSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("dialog-outline.png", 18); // 18px border matches game
            if (outlineSprite != null)
            {
                outlineImg.sprite = outlineSprite;
                outlineImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            outlineImg.color = new Color(0.996f, 0.353f, 0.086f, 1f); // Orange #FF6516 like IC10
            outlineImg.raycastTarget = false;
```

```
            // === Layer 2: Dark input area overlay - like IC10's PanelInputCode ===
            // KEY FINDING: IC10's PanelInputCode uses NO SPRITE - just flat black color!
            // This eliminates the shading artifacts we were seeing.
            
            var inputAreaGO = new GameObject("PanelInputCode");
            inputAreaGO.transform.SetParent(panel.transform, false);
            var inputAreaRect = inputAreaGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            inputAreaRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            inputAreaRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            inputAreaRect.offsetMin = new Vector2(6, 6);
            inputAreaRect.offsetMax = new Vector2(-6, -22);
            
            // NO SPRITE - flat color only (exactly like IC10's PanelInputCode from the game assets)
            var inputAreaBg = inputAreaGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            inputAreaBg.sprite = null; // No sprite - matches IC10
            inputAreaBg.color = new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, 0.75f); // #000000BF - black at 75%
            inputAreaBg.raycastTarget = true;
            
            // Orange outline for input area (matches IC10 theme)
            var inputOutlineGO = new GameObject("InputOutline");
            inputOutlineGO.transform.SetParent(inputAreaGO.transform, false);
            var inputOutlineRect = inputOutlineGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            inputOutlineRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            inputOutlineRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            inputOutlineRect.offsetMin = Vector2.zero;
            inputOutlineRect.offsetMax = Vector2.zero;
            var inputOutlineImg = inputOutlineGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            var inputOutlineSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("dialog-outline.png", 18); // 18px border
            if (inputOutlineSprite != null)
            {
                inputOutlineImg.sprite = inputOutlineSprite;
                inputOutlineImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            inputOutlineImg.color = new Color(0.996f, 0.353f, 0.086f, 1f); // Orange #FF6516
            inputOutlineImg.raycastTarget = false;
```

```
            // === Layer 2: Dark input area overlay - like IC10's PanelInputCode ===

            var inputAreaGO = new GameObject("PanelInputCode");
            inputAreaGO.transform.SetParent(panel.transform, false);
            var inputAreaRect = inputAreaGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            inputAreaRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            inputAreaRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            inputAreaRect.offsetMin = new Vector2(6, 6);
            inputAreaRect.offsetMax = new Vector2(-6, -22);
            
            // Layer 2a: Use dialog-bg sprite but colored black at 75% - this is the IC10 magic!

                inputAreaBg.sprite = inputAreaSprite;
                inputAreaBg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;

            inputAreaBg.color = new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, 0.75f); // #000000BF - black at 75% like IC10's PanelInputCode
```

```
            rect.anchorMax = new Vector2(0, 1);
            rect.pivot = new Vector2(0, 0.5f);
            rect.anchoredPosition = Vector2.zero;
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is saying:
1. The outlines were supposed to be black, not orange - I incorrectly changed them to orange
2. The "square thing" is bleeding out - likely because we're using no sprite, the corners are now square instead of rounded
3. They want the exact game implementation with child objects if needed

Looking at my research from the AssetRipper export, the IC10's PanelInputCode structure is:
- Root: NO sprite, flat black #000000BF
- Children: window-shadow → dialog-bg → outline

So the rounded corners come from CHILD elements, not the root. The root is just a flat color rectangle, and then children with sprites provide the visual rounded corners and outline.

Let me re-read what I found:

From PanelInputCode in Base.unity:
```yaml
m_Color: {r: 0, g: 0, b: 0, a: 0.7490196}  # BLACK ~75% opacity
m_Sprite: {fileID: 0}  # NO SPRITE
```

And it has children: fileIDs 10791, 10375, 10256, 10692, 10678

The structure should be:
```
PanelInputCode (NO SPRITE - flat black #000000BF)
├── window-shadow (sprite, probably white tinted)
│   └── dialog-bg (sprite with TranslucentImage, blue color)
│       └── outline (dialog-outline sprite)
└── [content...]
```

But wait, looking more carefully at the IC10 screenshot the user showed earlier - the inner PanelInputCode area doesn't have an orange outline, it has a subtle darker/black outline.

Let me implement the proper layered structure:

1. Root panel: NO sprite, flat color (for the outer container - handles sizing)
2. Child 1: window-shadow sprite (provides soft shadow)
3. Child 2: dialog-bg sprite (provides the rounded background)  


4. Child 3: dialog-outline sprite (provides the thin border)
5. Content inside

I'll carefully reconstruct the hierarchy to ensure the rounded corners and subtle border are correctly implemented through child sprites. The root remains a simple flat black container, with visual details emerging from strategically placed child elements.

The shading artifact is coming from the `dialog-bg.png` sprite itself - it has built-in highlights/gradients baked into the texture. When we tint it black at 75% opacity, those internal light areas still show through slightly.

The game gets around this by using **TranslucentImage** component (not regular Image) with special `spriteBlending`, `vibrancy`, and `brightness` settings that we can't easily replicate.

Let me try a different approach - **don't use dialog-bg for the inner panels at all**. Just use a flat color background with the dialog-outline providing the rounded border shape:

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is seeing the weird shading artifact again. They're saying IC10's PanelInputCode is rendered the same way but doesn't have the issue. 

Let me think about what could be different:

1. The dialog-bg sprite itself - maybe ours is different from the game's actual sprite?
2. The color values - maybe we're using the wrong color
3. The sprite loading - maybe our LoadSlicedSprite function creates a different sprite than the game uses

The key finding from the AssetRipper export was that IC10's PanelInputCode has:
- `m_Sprite: {fileID: 0}` - NO sprite on the root
- `m_Color: {r: 0, g: 0, b: 0, a: 0.7490196}` - black 75%

But we also saw that the dialog-bg when used elsewhere has TranslucentImage with special settings like `spriteBlending: 0.65`, `vibrancy`, `brightness`, etc.

The issue might be:
1. Our dialog-bg.png extracted sprite might have built-in gradients/highlights that the game's original doesn't show because they use TranslucentImage with special blending
2. Our sprite border might be wrong (we're using 18 but maybe that's not correct for how we're loading it)

Let me think about another approach - maybe we should:
1. Use a completely flat color with no sprite at all for the inner panels
2. NOT use dialog-bg for the inner dark areas
3. Only use dialog-outline for the border

Actually, looking back at the game data again:
- PanelInputCode root: NO SPRITE, flat black color
- Children provide the visual layering

But the children in IC10 are:
- window-shadow
- dialog-bg (with TranslucentImage)
- outline

The TranslucentImage is special - it has blur effects and special blending that we can't replicate without that component.


I see a straightforward solution to eliminate the shading artifact. By removing the dialog-bg sprite entirely and using a solid black color with the dialog-outline for borders, we can simplify the rendering. This approach mimics the game's PanelInputCode structure, avoiding complex sprite blending and potential visual inconsistencies.

```
        /// Load a 9-sliced sprite from the images folder
        /// </summary>
        private Sprite LoadSlicedSprite(string filename, int borderSize)
        {
            // Use the mod's image loading utility
            return StationpediaAscendedMod.LoadSlicedSprite(filename, borderSize);
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Load a regular sprite from the images folder
        /// </summary>
        private Sprite LoadSprite(string filename)
        {
            return StationpediaAscendedMod.LoadImageFromModFolder(filename);
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Get or create the TranslucentImage material by finding an existing one in the game's UI
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Find an existing TranslucentImage in the scene to steal its material
            var existingTranslucent = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectOfType<TranslucentImage>();
            if (existingTranslucent != null && existingTranslucent.material != null)
            {
                // Create a copy so we don't modify the original
                _translucentMaterial = new Material(existingTranslucent.material);
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log($"Found TranslucentImage material from existing UI");
                return _translucentMaterial;
            }
            
            // Fallback: Try to create material from shader
            var shader = Shader.Find("UI/TranslucentImage");
            if (shader != null)
            {
                _translucentMaterial = new Material(shader);
                // Set default values matching the game's Default-Translucent material
                _translucentMaterial.SetFloat("_Vibrancy", 2f);
                _translucentMaterial.SetFloat("_Brightness", -0.257f);
                _translucentMaterial.SetFloat("_Flatten", 0.2f);
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log($"Created TranslucentImage material from shader");
                return _translucentMaterial;
            }
            
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log($"Warning: Could not find TranslucentImage material or shader");
            return null;
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create a TranslucentImage component with proper settings, matching IC10's Background style
        /// </summary>
        private TranslucentImage CreateTranslucentImage(GameObject go, Sprite sprite, Color color, float spriteBlending = 1f)
        {
            var transImg = go.AddComponent<TranslucentImage>();
            
            // Set sprite and type
            if (sprite != null)
            {
                transImg.sprite = sprite;
                transImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            
            // Set color (the game uses dark blue tint, not black)
            transImg.color = color;
            
            // TranslucentImage-specific properties (matching IC10's Background: 16457)
            transImg.vibrancy = 2f;
            transImg.brightness = -0.257f;
            transImg.flatten = 0.2f;
            transImg.spriteBlending = spriteBlending;
            
            // Apply the material
            var mat = GetTranslucentMaterial();
            if (mat != null)
            {
                transImg.material = mat;
            }
            
            return transImg;
        }
```

```
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material by finding an existing one in the game's UI.
        /// Returns null if not found - caller should fall back to regular Image.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Find ALL TranslucentImage components in the scene
            var allTranslucent = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<TranslucentImage>();
            foreach (var existing in allTranslucent)
            {
                // Check materialForRendering first (what actually gets rendered)
                if (existing.materialForRendering != null && 
                    existing.materialForRendering.shader != null &&
                    existing.materialForRendering.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = existing.materialForRendering;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material from {existing.gameObject.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
                
                // Also check the regular material property
                if (existing.material != null && 
                    existing.material.shader != null &&
                    existing.material.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = existing.material;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material (material) from {existing.gameObject.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Material not available - will use regular Image fallback
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning("TranslucentImage material not found - using regular Image fallback");
            return null;
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create a panel background with TranslucentImage effect if available, otherwise regular Image.
        /// This gracefully falls back if the TranslucentImage shader/material isn't available.
        /// </summary>
        private Image CreatePanelBackground(GameObject go, Sprite sprite, Color color)
        {
            // Try to get the TranslucentImage material
            var mat = GetTranslucentMaterial();
            
            if (mat != null)
            {
                // We have the material - use TranslucentImage for the blur effect
                var transImg = go.AddComponent<TranslucentImage>();
                
                if (sprite != null)
                {
                    transImg.sprite = sprite;
                    transImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
                }
                
                transImg.color = color;
                
                // TranslucentImage-specific properties (matching IC10's Background)
                transImg.vibrancy = 2f;
                transImg.brightness = -0.257f;
                transImg.flatten = 0.2f;
                transImg.spriteBlending = 1f;
                transImg.material = mat;
                
                return transImg;
            }
            else
            {
                // Fallback: Use regular Image (no blur effect, but no errors either)
                var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
                
                if (sprite != null)
                {
                    img.sprite = sprite;
                    img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
                }
                
                img.color = color;
                return img;
            }
        }
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The error shows that:
1. The material isn't being found or created properly
2. It's falling back to "Default UI Material" which doesn't have the TranslucentImage shader properties

The problem is that both `FindObjectOfType<TranslucentImage>()` and `Shader.Find("UI/TranslucentImage")` are returning null, so we end up with no material being set on our TranslucentImage components.

Let me think about this more carefully:
1. `FindObjectOfType<TranslucentImage>()` - This might not find anything if the game's UI isn't loaded yet, or if no TranslucentImage exists in the scene at that moment
2. `Shader.Find("UI/TranslucentImage")` - This might fail because the shader name might be different, or because shaders need to be in a Resources folder or referenced by a material to be included in builds

Looking at the TranslucentImage code:
```csharp
correctShader = Shader.Find("UI/TranslucentImage");
```

So the shader name IS "UI/TranslucentImage". But Shader.Find() only works for shaders that are:
1. In a Resources folder
2. Included in "Always Included Shaders" in graphics settings
3. Referenced by a material that's used somewhere

Since we can't add shaders, we need to find an existing TranslucentImage's material at runtime. The issue is WHEN we're looking - the game's UI might not be loaded yet.
