```
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material by searching all loaded materials.
        /// Returns null if not found - caller should fall back to regular Image.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Method 1: Search ALL loaded materials for one with the TranslucentImage shader
            // This finds the material even if no TranslucentImage components exist yet
            var allMaterials = Resources.FindObjectsOfTypeAll<Material>();
            foreach (var mat in allMaterials)
            {
                if (mat != null && mat.shader != null && mat.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = mat;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material: {mat.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Method 2: Find from existing TranslucentImage components
            var allTranslucent = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<TranslucentImage>();
            foreach (var existing in allTranslucent)
            {
                if (existing.materialForRendering != null && 
                    existing.materialForRendering.shader != null &&
                    existing.materialForRendering.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = existing.materialForRendering;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material from component: {existing.gameObject.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Method 3: Try to create from shader as last resort
            var shader = Shader.Find("UI/TranslucentImage");
            if (shader != null)
            {
                _translucentMaterial = new Material(shader);
                _translucentMaterial.SetFloat("_Vibrancy", 2f);
                _translucentMaterial.SetFloat("_Brightness", -0.257f);
                _translucentMaterial.SetFloat("_Flatten", 0.2f);
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo("Created new TranslucentImage material from shader");
                return _translucentMaterial;
            }
            
            // Material not available - will use regular Image fallback
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning("TranslucentImage material not found - using regular Image fallback");
            return null;
        }
```

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material by finding an existing one in the game's UI.
        /// Returns null if not found - caller should fall back to regular Image.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Find ALL TranslucentImage components in the scene
            var allTranslucent = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<TranslucentImage>();
            foreach (var existing in allTranslucent)
            {
                // Check materialForRendering first (what actually gets rendered)
                if (existing.materialForRendering != null && 
                    existing.materialForRendering.shader != null &&
                    existing.materialForRendering.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = existing.materialForRendering;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material from {existing.gameObject.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
                
                // Also check the regular material property
                if (existing.material != null && 
                    existing.material.shader != null &&
                    existing.material.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = existing.material;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material (material) from {existing.gameObject.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Material not available - will use regular Image fallback
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning("TranslucentImage material not found - using regular Image fallback");
            return null;
        }
```

```
        public static void DumpTranslucentImageInfo()
        {
            UnityEngine.Debug.Log("=== TRANSLUCENT IMAGE DEBUG DUMP ===");
            
            // Find all TranslucentImageSources
            var sources = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImageSource>();
            UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"TranslucentImageSources found: {sources.Length}");
            foreach (var src in sources)

                var cam = src.GetComponent<Camera>();
                if (cam != null)
                {
                    UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"    Camera: {cam.name}, depth={cam.depth}");
                }
            }
            
            // Find all TranslucentImages
            var images = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImage>();
            UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"TranslucentImages found: {images.Length}");
            foreach (var ti in images)
            {

                UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"    vibrancy={ti.vibrancy:F3}, brightness={ti.brightness:F3}");
                UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"    flatten={ti.flatten:F3}, spriteBlending={ti.spriteBlending:F3}");
                UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"    color={ti.color}");
                UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"    material={ti.material?.name ?? "null"}, shader={ti.material?.shader?.name ?? "null"}");
                UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"    materialForRendering={ti.materialForRendering?.name ?? "null"}");
                if (ti.sprite != null)
                {
                    UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"    sprite={ti.sprite.name}, border={ti.sprite.border}");
                }
            }
            
            UnityEngine.Debug.Log("=== END TRANSLUCENT IMAGE DUMP ===");
        }
```

The user is saying the hot reload isn't working for the UIAssetInspector changes. This is a common issue with BepInEx script reloading - the old assembly stays loaded in memory, and when you call methods, it still uses the old code.

The problem is that BepInEx's ScriptLoader reloads assemblies, but Unity/Mono doesn't truly unload the old assembly - it stays in memory. So when the static `UIAssetInspector.Toggle()` is called, it might be calling the old assembly's code.

The key issue is that the `assetdisplay` command handler is in the main plugin, and it calls `UIAssetInspector.Toggle()`. If the command handler itself is in the old assembly, it will call the old `UIAssetInspector` class.

Actually, the fundamental issue is that in .NET/Mono, you can't truly unload an assembly without unloading the entire AppDomain. BepInEx's script reloading works by loading the new assembly alongside the old one, and the new types have different type identities.

One workaround is to use reflection to find and call methods on the new assembly's types, but that's complex.

A simpler approach for debugging would be to:

I'll explore potential workarounds for script reloading limitations. BepInEx's assembly loading can be tricky, with the old code persisting even after apparent updates. Reflection might offer a complex solution, but a restart is likely the most straightforward approach to ensure the latest code runs correctly.

The core challenge involves C#'s AppDomain constraints, preventing true dynamic assembly unloading. This means game modifications often require a full restart to guarantee fresh code execution.

The specific issue centers on component instantiation during hot reload. When creating a new GameObject, the type reference comes from the currently loaded assembly - which might be the old, unmodified version.

This creates a subtle problem where the `Initialize()` method potentially adds an outdated component type, even after attempting to reload the plugin. The complexity increases when considering how BepInEx manages plugin instances during runtime.

Practically speaking, developers should expect to restart the game when making significant code changes to ensure clean type resolution and component initialization.

```
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Text;
using UnityEngine;
using UnityEngine.UI;
using UnityEngine.EventSystems;
using TMPro;
using Assets.Scripts.UI;

namespace StationpediaAscended.Diagnostics
{
    /// <summary>
    /// Debug tool to inspect UI assets under the mouse cursor.
    /// Toggle with console command: assetdisplay
    /// Uses IMGUI for a scrollable, resizable window.
    /// </summary>
    public class UIAssetInspector : MonoBehaviour
    {
        public static UIAssetInspector Instance { get; private set; }
        public static bool IsEnabled { get; private set; } = false;
        
        private PointerEventData _pointerData;
        private List<RaycastResult> _raycastResults = new List<RaycastResult>();
        private StringBuilder _sb = new StringBuilder();
        
        // IMGUI state
        private Rect _windowRect = new Rect(20, 20, 500, 600);
        private Vector2 _scrollPosition = Vector2.zero;
        private string _cachedContent = "";
        private float _lastUpdateTime = 0f;
        private const float UPDATE_INTERVAL = 0.1f; // Update 10 times per second
        
        // Styles
        private GUIStyle _windowStyle;
        private GUIStyle _labelStyle;
        private GUIStyle _headerStyle;
        private GUIStyle _subHeaderStyle;
        private bool _stylesInitialized = false;
        
        public static void Initialize()
        {
            // Find and destroy any existing UIAssetInspector by name (handles hot reload)
            var existingGO = GameObject.Find("UIAssetInspector");
            if (existingGO != null)
            {
                Destroy(existingGO);
                UnityEngine.Debug.Log("[UIAssetInspector] Destroyed old instance for hot reload");
            }
            Instance = null;
            
            var go = new GameObject("UIAssetInspector");
            Instance = go.AddComponent<UIAssetInspector>();
            DontDestroyOnLoad(go);
            
            UnityEngine.Debug.Log("[UIAssetInspector] Initialized. Use 'assetdisplay' command to toggle.");
        }
        
        public static void Toggle()
        {
            // Always reinitialize on toggle to pick up hot reload changes
            Initialize();
            Instance.SetEnabled(!IsEnabled);
        }
        
        public static void Cleanup()
        {
            if (Instance != null)
            {
                Destroy(Instance.gameObject);
                Instance = null;
            }
        }
        
        private void SetEnabled(bool enabled)
        {
            IsEnabled = enabled;
            UnityEngine.Debug.Log($"[UIAssetInspector] {(enabled ? "ENABLED" : "DISABLED")}");
        }
        
        private void InitStyles()
        {
            if (_stylesInitialized) return;
            
            _windowStyle = new GUIStyle(GUI.skin.window);
            _windowStyle.normal.background = MakeTexture(2, 2, new Color(0.05f, 0.08f, 0.12f, 0.95f));
            
            _labelStyle = new GUIStyle(GUI.skin.label);
            _labelStyle.fontSize = 12;
            _labelStyle.normal.textColor = new Color(0.9f, 0.9f, 0.9f);
            _labelStyle.richText = true;
            _labelStyle.wordWrap = true;
            
            _headerStyle = new GUIStyle(_labelStyle);
            _headerStyle.fontSize = 14;
            _headerStyle.fontStyle = FontStyle.Bold;
            _headerStyle.normal.textColor = new Color(0.98f, 0.69f, 0.23f); // Orange
            
            _subHeaderStyle = new GUIStyle(_labelStyle);
            _subHeaderStyle.fontSize = 13;
            _subHeaderStyle.fontStyle = FontStyle.Bold;
            _subHeaderStyle.normal.textColor = new Color(1f, 0.84f, 0f); // Gold
            
            _stylesInitialized = true;
        }
        
        private Texture2D MakeTexture(int width, int height, Color color)
        {
            Color[] pixels = new Color[width * height];
            for (int i = 0; i < pixels.Length; i++)
                pixels[i] = color;
            
            Texture2D tex = new Texture2D(width, height);
            tex.SetPixels(pixels);
            tex.Apply();
            return tex;
        }
        
        void Update()
        {
            if (!IsEnabled) return;
            
            // Throttle updates
            if (Time.unscaledTime - _lastUpdateTime < UPDATE_INTERVAL) return;
            _lastUpdateTime = Time.unscaledTime;
            
            UpdateContent();
        }
        
        private void UpdateContent()
        {
            _sb.Clear();
            
            // Check for TranslucentImageSources globally
            var tiSources = FindObjectsOfType<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImageSource>();
            if (tiSources.Length > 0)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FF80FF>TranslucentImageSources: {tiSources.Length}</color>");
                foreach (var tis in tiSources)
                {
                    _sb.AppendLine($"  -> {tis.gameObject.name}");
                }
                _sb.AppendLine("------------------------");
            }
            else
            {
                _sb.AppendLine("<color=#FF8080>No TranslucentImageSource in scene!</color>");
                _sb.AppendLine("------------------------");
            }
            
            var eventSystem = EventSystem.current;
            if (eventSystem == null)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine("<color=#FF8080>No EventSystem found</color>");
                _cachedContent = _sb.ToString();
                return;
            }
            
            if (_pointerData == null)
                _pointerData = new PointerEventData(eventSystem);
            
            _pointerData.position = Input.mousePosition;
            _raycastResults.Clear();
            
            eventSystem.RaycastAll(_pointerData, _raycastResults);
            
            // Filter out our own inspector
            var filteredResults = new List<RaycastResult>();
            foreach (var result in _raycastResults)
            {
                if (result.gameObject != null && !result.gameObject.transform.IsChildOf(transform))
                    filteredResults.Add(result);
            }
            
            if (filteredResults.Count == 0)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine("<color=#FFFF80>No UI elements under cursor</color>");
                _cachedContent = _sb.ToString();
                return;
            }
            
            _sb.AppendLine($"<color=#80FFFF>Found {filteredResults.Count} element(s)</color>\n");
            
            foreach (var result in filteredResults)
            {
                AppendGameObjectInfo(result.gameObject);
            }
            
            _cachedContent = _sb.ToString();
        }
        
        private void AppendGameObjectInfo(GameObject go)
        {
            _sb.AppendLine($"<color=#FFD700><b>[{go.name}]</b></color>");
            
            // Get hierarchy path
            string path = GetHierarchyPath(go.transform, 5);
            _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#A0A0A0>Path: {path}</color>");
            
            // RectTransform
            var rect = go.GetComponent<RectTransform>();
            if (rect != null)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#C080C0>RectTransform:</color>");
                _sb.AppendLine($"    sizeDelta: {rect.sizeDelta}");
                _sb.AppendLine($"    anchors: {rect.anchorMin} - {rect.anchorMax}");
                _sb.AppendLine($"    pivot: {rect.pivot}");
            }
            
            // Image
            var image = go.GetComponent<Image>();
            if (image != null)
            {
                string spriteName = image.sprite != null ? image.sprite.name : "(no sprite)";
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#80FF80>Image: {spriteName}</color>");
                
                if (image.sprite != null)
                {
                    var tex = image.sprite.texture;
                    if (tex != null)
                        _sb.AppendLine($"    Texture: {tex.name} ({tex.width}x{tex.height})");
                    
                    var border = image.sprite.border;
                    if (border != Vector4.zero)
                        _sb.AppendLine($"    Border(9-slice): L={border.x} B={border.y} R={border.z} T={border.w}");
                }
                
                _sb.AppendLine($"    Color: {ColorToHex(image.color)}");
                _sb.AppendLine($"    Type: {image.type}");
                _sb.AppendLine($"    Material: {(image.material != null ? image.material.name : "null")}");
                _sb.AppendLine($"    materialForRendering: {(image.materialForRendering != null ? image.materialForRendering.name : "null")}");
                
                // TranslucentImage
                var ti = go.GetComponent<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImage>();
                if (ti != null)
                {
                    _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FF80FF>== TranslucentImage ==</color>");
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    vibrancy: {ti.vibrancy:F3}");
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    brightness: {ti.brightness:F3}");
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    flatten: {ti.flatten:F3}");
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    spriteBlending: {ti.spriteBlending:F3}");
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    TI.material: {(ti.material != null ? ti.material.name : "null")}");
                    if (ti.material != null && ti.material.shader != null)
                        _sb.AppendLine($"    TI.shader: {ti.material.shader.name}");
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    TI.materialForRendering: {(ti.materialForRendering != null ? ti.materialForRendering.name : "null")}");
                }
            }
            
            // RawImage
            var rawImage = go.GetComponent<RawImage>();
            if (rawImage != null)
            {
                string texName = rawImage.texture != null ? rawImage.texture.name : "(no texture)";
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#80FF80>RawImage: {texName}</color>");
            }
            
            // TextMeshProUGUI
            var tmp = go.GetComponent<TextMeshProUGUI>();
            if (tmp != null)
            {
                string textPreview = tmp.text ?? "";
                if (textPreview.Length > 30)
                    textPreview = textPreview.Substring(0, 30) + "...";
                textPreview = textPreview.Replace("\n", "\\n").Replace("<", "[").Replace(">", "]");
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#80FFFF>TMP: \"{textPreview}\"</color>");
                if (tmp.font != null)
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    Font: {tmp.font.name}");
            }
            
            // Canvas
            var canvas = go.GetComponent<Canvas>();
            if (canvas != null)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#8080FF>Canvas: sortOrder={canvas.sortingOrder}, mode={canvas.renderMode}</color>");
                _sb.AppendLine($"    additionalShaderChannels: {canvas.additionalShaderChannels}");
                
                var rootCanvas = canvas.rootCanvas;
                if (rootCanvas != null && rootCanvas.worldCamera != null)
                {
                    var cam = rootCanvas.worldCamera;
                    var tiSource = cam.GetComponent<LeTai.Asset.TranslucentImage.TranslucentImageSource>();
                    if (tiSource != null)
                        _sb.AppendLine($"    <color=#FF80FF>TranslucentImageSource on {cam.name}</color>");
                }
            }
            
            // CanvasGroup
            var canvasGroup = go.GetComponent<CanvasGroup>();
            if (canvasGroup != null)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#8080FF>CanvasGroup: alpha={canvasGroup.alpha}</color>");
            }
            
            // Button
            var button = go.GetComponent<Button>();
            if (button != null)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFFF80>Button</color>");
            }
            
            // Shadow/Outline
            var shadow = go.GetComponent<Shadow>();
            if (shadow != null)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFA0A0>Shadow: {ColorToHex(shadow.effectColor)}, dist={shadow.effectDistance}</color>");
            }
            
            var outline = go.GetComponent<Outline>();
            if (outline != null)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFA0A0>Outline: {ColorToHex(outline.effectColor)}, dist={outline.effectDistance}</color>");
            }
            
            // Children summary
            int childCount = go.transform.childCount;
            if (childCount > 0)
            {
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#FFAA00>Children: {childCount}</color>");
                for (int i = 0; i < Mathf.Min(childCount, 5); i++)
                {
                    var child = go.transform.GetChild(i);
                    var childImg = child.GetComponent<Image>();
                    string childSprite = childImg != null && childImg.sprite != null ? childImg.sprite.name : "";
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    {child.name} {(string.IsNullOrEmpty(childSprite) ? "" : "-> " + childSprite)}");
                }
                if (childCount > 5)
                    _sb.AppendLine($"    ... and {childCount - 5} more");
            }
            
            // Parent info
            if (go.transform.parent != null)
            {
                var parentGO = go.transform.parent.gameObject;
                var parentImage = parentGO.GetComponent<Image>();
                string parentSprite = parentImage != null && parentImage.sprite != null ? parentImage.sprite.name : "";
                _sb.AppendLine($"  <color=#60A060>Parent: {parentGO.name} {(string.IsNullOrEmpty(parentSprite) ? "" : "-> " + parentSprite)}</color>");
            }
            
            _sb.AppendLine();
        }
        
        void OnGUI()
        {
            if (!IsEnabled) return;
            
            InitStyles();
            
            // Make window draggable and resizable
            _windowRect = GUILayout.Window(
                98765, // Unique ID
                _windowRect,
                DrawWindow,
                "UI Asset Inspector",
                _windowStyle,
                GUILayout.MinWidth(300),
                GUILayout.MinHeight(200)
            );
            
            // Clamp to screen
            _windowRect.x = Mathf.Clamp(_windowRect.x, 0, Screen.width - 100);
            _windowRect.y = Mathf.Clamp(_windowRect.y, 0, Screen.height - 100);
        }
        
        private void DrawWindow(int windowId)
        {
            // Header
            GUILayout.Label("== UI ASSET INSPECTOR ==", _headerStyle);
            GUILayout.Label("Hover over UI elements to inspect", _labelStyle);
            GUILayout.Space(5);
            
            // Scrollable content
            _scrollPosition = GUILayout.BeginScrollView(_scrollPosition, GUILayout.ExpandHeight(true));
            
            GUILayout.Label(_cachedContent, _labelStyle);
            
            GUILayout.EndScrollView();
            
            // Close button
            GUILayout.Space(5);
            if (GUILayout.Button("Close"))
            {
                SetEnabled(false);
            }
            
            // Make window draggable
            GUI.DragWindow(new Rect(0, 0, _windowRect.width, 20));
        }
        
        private string GetHierarchyPath(Transform t, int maxDepth)
        {
            if (t == null || maxDepth <= 0) return "";
            
            var parts = new List<string>();
            var current = t;
            int depth = 0;
            
            while (current != null && depth < maxDepth)
            {
                parts.Insert(0, current.name);
                current = current.parent;
                depth++;
            }
            
            if (current != null)
                parts.Insert(0, "...");
            
            return string.Join("/", parts);
        }
        
        private string ColorToHex(Color c)
        {
            return $"#{ColorUtility.ToHtmlStringRGBA(c)}";
        }
    }
}
```

```
            {

            }
            
            // Raycast to find UI elements first (to know how many columns we need)
            UpdateTooltipContent();
            
            // Force layout update to get accurate size
            LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(_tooltipRect);
            
            // Position tooltip near mouse with smart screen boundary handling
            PositionTooltip();

        private void CopyToClipboard()
        {
            if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(_lastPlainTextContent)) return;
            
            GUIUtility.systemCopyBuffer = _lastPlainTextContent;
            UnityEngine.Debug.Log("[UIAssetInspector] Copied to clipboard!");
        }
        
        private void PositionTooltip()
        {
            Vector2 mousePos = Input.mousePosition;
            Vector2 size = _tooltipRect.sizeDelta;
            
            // Default position: to the right and below cursor
            float tooltipX = mousePos.x + 20;
            float tooltipY = mousePos.y - 20;
            
            // Check right edge - if tooltip goes off screen, flip to left side of cursor
            if (tooltipX + size.x > Screen.width - 10)
            {
                tooltipX = mousePos.x - size.x - 20;
            }
            
            // Check left edge - if still off screen (very wide tooltip), clamp to left edge
            if (tooltipX < 10)
            {
                tooltipX = 10;
            }
            
            // Check bottom edge - if tooltip goes off screen, flip to above cursor
            if (tooltipY - size.y < 10)
            {
                tooltipY = mousePos.y + size.y + 20;
            }
            
            // Check top edge - if still off screen (very tall tooltip), clamp to top
            if (tooltipY > Screen.height - 10)
            {
                tooltipY = Screen.height - 10;
            }
            
            _tooltipRect.position = new Vector2(tooltipX, tooltipY);
        } oldString:        void Update()

        }
        
        private void BuildPlainTextContent(int columnsNeeded)
        {
            _sb.Clear();
            for (int i = 0; i < columnsNeeded; i++)
            {
                // Strip rich text tags for plain clipboard content
                string text = _columnBuilders[i].ToString();
                text = System.Text.RegularExpressions.Regex.Replace(text, "<[^>]+>", "");
                _sb.Append(text);
            }
            _lastPlainTextContent = _sb.ToString();
        }
        
        private void ApplyColumnsToUI(int columnsNeeded)
        {
            // Calculate available width and adjust columns to fit
            float availableWidth = Screen.width - PADDING * 2;
            float totalNeededWidth = columnsNeeded * COLUMN_WIDTH + (columnsNeeded - 1) * COLUMN_SPACING + PADDING * 2;
            
            // If too wide, reduce columns or shrink width
            int actualColumns = columnsNeeded;
            float columnWidth = COLUMN_WIDTH;
            
            if (totalNeededWidth > availableWidth)
            {
                // First try reducing columns
                while (actualColumns > 1)
                {
                    float testWidth = actualColumns * COLUMN_WIDTH + (actualColumns - 1) * COLUMN_SPACING + PADDING * 2;
                    if (testWidth <= availableWidth) break;
                    actualColumns--;
                }
                
                // If still too wide with one column, shrink the column width
                if (actualColumns == 1 && COLUMN_WIDTH + PADDING * 2 > availableWidth)
                {
                    columnWidth = availableWidth - PADDING * 2 - 20;
                }
            }
            
            for (int i = 0; i < _columnTexts.Count; i++)
            {
                bool shouldShow = i < actualColumns;
                _columnTexts[i].gameObject.SetActive(shouldShow);
                
                if (shouldShow)
                {
                    _columnTexts[i].text = _columnBuilders[i].ToString();
                    _columnLayoutElements[i].minWidth = columnWidth;
                    _columnLayoutElements[i].preferredWidth = columnWidth;
                }
            }
        } oldString:            ApplyColumnsToUI(columnsNeeded);
        }
        
        private void ApplyColumnsToUI(int columnsNeeded)

            {
                bool shouldShow = i < columnsNeeded;
                _columnTexts[i].gameObject.SetActive(shouldShow);
                if (shouldShow)
                {
                    _columnTexts[i].text = _columnBuilders[i].ToString();
                }
            }
            LayoutRebuilder.ForceRebuildLayoutImmediate(_tooltipRect);
```

```
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            var allMaterials = Resources.FindObjectsOfTypeAll<Material>();
            
            // Method 1: Search for "Default-Translucent" material by name (IC10's exact material)
            foreach (var mat in allMaterials)
            {
                if (mat != null && mat.name == "Default-Translucent")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = mat;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found Default-Translucent material!");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Method 2: Search ALL loaded materials for one with the TranslucentImage shader

            {
                if (mat != null && mat.shader != null && mat.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = mat;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material by shader: {mat.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Method 3: Find from existing TranslucentImage components

                    _translucentMaterial = existing.material;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material from component: {existing.gameObject.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Material not available - will use regular Image fallback
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning("TranslucentImage material not found - using regular Image fallback");

        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
```

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material by searching all loaded materials.
        /// Returns null if not found - caller should fall back to regular Image.
        /// NOTE: This is expensive and should only be called after the game is fully loaded.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            // Return cached material if already found
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Don't search during initialization - it can hang the game
            // The material search will happen when TryUpgradeToTranslucentImage is called
            return null;
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Attempt to find and cache the TranslucentImage material.
        /// Call this after the game is fully loaded (e.g., when window is shown).
        /// </summary>
        private void TryFindTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return;
            
            try
            {
                // Method 1: Find from existing TranslucentImage components first (safest)
                var allTranslucent = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<TranslucentImage>();
                foreach (var existing in allTranslucent)
                {
                    if (existing.material != null)
                    {
                        _translucentMaterial = existing.material;
                        StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found material from existing TranslucentImage: {_translucentMaterial.name}");
                        return;
                    }
                }
                
                // Method 2: Search loaded materials by name
                var allMaterials = Resources.FindObjectsOfTypeAll<Material>();
                foreach (var mat in allMaterials)
                {
                    if (mat != null && mat.name == "Default-Translucent")
                    {
                        _translucentMaterial = mat;
                        StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found Default-Translucent material!");
                        return;
                    }
                }
                
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning("TranslucentImage material not found");
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning($"Error searching for TranslucentImage material: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create a panel background - always uses regular Image during creation.
        /// TranslucentImage upgrade can be done later via TryUpgradeToTranslucentImage.
        /// </summary>
        private Image CreatePanelBackground(GameObject go, Sprite sprite, Color color)
        {
            // Always use regular Image during creation to avoid hanging during initialization
            var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
            
            if (sprite != null)
            {
                img.sprite = sprite;
                img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            
            img.color = color;
            return img;
```

```
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material by searching all loaded materials.
        /// Returns null if not found - caller should fall back to regular Image.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            var allMaterials = Resources.FindObjectsOfTypeAll<Material>();
            
            // Method 1: Search for "Default-Translucent" by exact name (IC10's material)
            foreach (var mat in allMaterials)
            {
                if (mat != null && mat.name == "Default-Translucent")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = mat;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found Default-Translucent material!");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Method 2: Search by shader name as fallback
            foreach (var mat in allMaterials)
            {
                if (mat != null && mat.shader != null && mat.shader.name == "UI/TranslucentImage")
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = mat;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found TranslucentImage material by shader: {mat.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Method 3: Find from existing TranslucentImage components (get .material not .materialForRendering)
            var allTranslucent = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<TranslucentImage>();
            foreach (var existing in allTranslucent)
            {
                if (existing.material != null)
                {
                    _translucentMaterial = existing.material;
                    StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo($"Found material from existing TranslucentImage: {_translucentMaterial.name}");
                    return _translucentMaterial;
                }
            }
            
            // Material not available - will use regular Image fallback
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogWarning("TranslucentImage material not found - using regular Image fallback");
            return null;
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create a panel background with TranslucentImage effect if available, otherwise regular Image.
        /// This gracefully falls back if the TranslucentImage shader/material isn't available.
        /// </summary>
        private Image CreatePanelBackground(GameObject go, Sprite sprite, Color color)
        {
            // Try to get the TranslucentImage material
            var mat = GetTranslucentMaterial();
            
            if (mat != null)
            {
                // We have the material - use TranslucentImage for the blur effect
                var transImg = go.AddComponent<TranslucentImage>();
                
                if (sprite != null)
                {
                    transImg.sprite = sprite;
                    transImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
                }
                
                transImg.color = color;
                
                // TranslucentImage-specific properties (matching IC10's Background)
                transImg.vibrancy = 2f;
                transImg.brightness = -0.257f;
                transImg.flatten = 0.2f;
                transImg.spriteBlending = 1f;
                transImg.material = mat;
                
                return transImg;
```

```
        /// Attempt to find the TranslucentImage material and upgrade background Images.
        /// Call this after the game is fully loaded (e.g., when window is first shown).
        /// </summary>
        private void TryUpgradeToTranslucentImage()
        {
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return;
            
            try
            {
                // Method 1: Find from existing TranslucentImage components (IC10 should be loaded by now)
                var allTranslucent = UnityEngine.Object.FindObjectsOfType<TranslucentImage>();
                foreach (var existing in allTranslucent)
                {

                            break;

                }
            }
            catch (System.Exception ex)
            {
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogError($"[StationPlanner] Error upgrading to TranslucentImage: {ex.Message}");
            }
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Upgrade a regular Image to TranslucentImage by copying properties and replacing component.
        /// </summary>
        private void UpgradeImageToTranslucent(Image img)
        {
            if (img == null || _translucentMaterial == null) return;
            
            // Store properties
            var go = img.gameObject;
            var sprite = img.sprite;
            var color = img.color;
            var type = img.type;
            var raycast = img.raycastTarget;
            
            // Remove old Image component
            Destroy(img);
            
            // Add TranslucentImage component
            var transImg = go.AddComponent<TranslucentImage>();
            transImg.sprite = sprite;
            transImg.color = color;
            transImg.type = type;
            transImg.raycastTarget = raycast;
            
            // Set TranslucentImage properties to match IC10
            transImg.vibrancy = 2f;
            transImg.brightness = -0.257f;
            transImg.flatten = 0.2f;
            transImg.spriteBlending = 1f;
            transImg.material = _translucentMaterial;
        } oldString:        /// <summary>

```

```
  Material: Default-Translucent  <- THIS is the blur material on the TranslucentImage
  == TranslucentImage ==
    vibrancy: 2.000
    brightness: -0.257
    ...
```

So the issue is:
1. PanelInputCode itself has "Default UI Material" - this is just the regular material
2. The CHILD element at PanelInputCode/Image/Image has "Default-Translucent" material with TranslucentImage component
3. The "5 children" are other windows (help panels) that are siblings, not the blur panels

The user is misunderstanding - the outer PanelInputCode is just a canvas container. The blur effect is on the inner Image child which uses "Default-Translucent" material.

Let me explain this clearly and then implement the solution properly - we need to create TranslucentImage on the BACKGROUND panels (like FileTree and the Editor input area), not on the root canvas.

Actually, looking at our current code, we ARE creating backgrounds on the right elements (FileTree panel and inputAreaGO in the editor). The issue is we're not finding the Default-Translucent material because we removed the search.

The real problem: I removed the material search to fix the hang, but now we have no blur effect at all. We need a better approach - delay the material search until AFTER the window is first shown, when IC10 has already loaded its materials.

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material. Creates it from shader if needed.
        /// Returns null if shader not found.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            // Return cached material if already created
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Find the UI/TranslucentImage shader (built into the game)
            var shader = Shader.Find("UI/TranslucentImage");
            if (shader == null)
            {
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogError("[StationPlanner] UI/TranslucentImage shader not found!");
                return null;
            }
            
            // Create material from shader with IC10's exact settings
            _translucentMaterial = new Material(shader);
            _translucentMaterial.name = "StationPlanner-Translucent";
            
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo("[StationPlanner] Created TranslucentImage material from shader");
            return _translucentMaterial;
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create a panel background - always uses regular Image during creation.
        /// TranslucentImage upgrade can be done later via TryUpgradeToTranslucentImage.
        /// </summary>
        private Image CreatePanelBackground(GameObject go, Sprite sprite, Color color)
        {
            // Check if we can use TranslucentImage
            var mat = GetTranslucentMaterial();
            
            if (mat != null)
            {
                // Use TranslucentImage with the material we created
                var transImg = go.AddComponent<TranslucentImage>();
                
                if (sprite != null)
                {
                    transImg.sprite = sprite;
                    transImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
                }
                
                transImg.color = color;
                
                // IC10's exact TranslucentImage properties
                transImg.vibrancy = 2f;
                transImg.brightness = -0.257f;
                transImg.flatten = 0.2f;
                transImg.spriteBlending = 1f;
                transImg.material = mat;
                
                return transImg;
            }
            
            // Fallback to regular Image if shader not found
            var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
            
            if (sprite != null)
            {
                img.sprite = sprite;
                img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            
            img.color = color;
            return img;
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material by searching all loaded materials.
        /// Returns null if not found - caller should fall back to regular Image.
        /// NOTE: This is expensive and should only be called after the game is fully loaded.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            // Return cached material if already found
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Don't search during initialization - it can hang the game
            // The material search will happen when TryUpgradeToTranslucentImage is called
            return null;
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create a panel background - always uses regular Image during creation.
        /// TranslucentImage upgrade can be done later via TryUpgradeToTranslucentImage.
        /// </summary>
        private Image CreatePanelBackground(GameObject go, Sprite sprite, Color color)
        {
            // Always use regular Image during creation to avoid hanging during initialization
            var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
            
            if (sprite != null)
            {
                img.sprite = sprite;
                img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            
            img.color = color;
            return img;
        }
```

- newString:

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material. Creates it from shader if needed.
        /// Returns null if shader not found.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            // Return cached material if already created
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Find the UI/TranslucentImage shader (built into the game)
            var shader = Shader.Find("UI/TranslucentImage");
            if (shader == null)
            {
                StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogError("[StationPlanner] UI/TranslucentImage shader not found!");
                return null;
            }
            
            // Create material from shader
            _translucentMaterial = new Material(shader);
            _translucentMaterial.name = "StationPlanner-Translucent";
            
            StationpediaAscendedMod.Log?.LogInfo("[StationPlanner] Created TranslucentImage material from shader");
            return _translucentMaterial;
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create a panel background with TranslucentImage if shader is available.
        /// </summary>
        private Image CreatePanelBackground(GameObject go, Sprite sprite, Color color)
        {
            // Try to get/create the material from shader
            var mat = GetTranslucentMaterial();
            
            if (mat != null)
            {
                // Use TranslucentImage with the material we created
                var transImg = go.AddComponent<TranslucentImage>();
                
                if (sprite != null)
                {
                    transImg.sprite = sprite;
                    transImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
                }
                
                transImg.color = color;
                
                // IC10's exact TranslucentImage properties
                transImg.vibrancy = 2f;
                transImg.brightness = -0.257f;
                transImg.flatten = 0.2f;
                transImg.spriteBlending = 1f;
                transImg.material = mat;
                
                return transImg;
            }
            
            // Fallback to regular Image if shader not found
            var img = go.AddComponent<Image>();
            
            if (sprite != null)
            {
                img.sprite = sprite;
                img.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            
            img.color = color;
            return img;
        }
```

- oldString:

```
        /// <summary>
        /// Get the TranslucentImage material by searching all loaded materials.
        /// Returns null if not found - caller should fall back to regular Image.
        /// NOTE: This is expensive and should only be called after the game is fully loaded.
        /// </summary>
        private Material GetTranslucentMaterial()
        {
            // Return cached material if already found
            if (_translucentMaterial != null) return _translucentMaterial;
            
            // Don't search during initialization - it can hang the game
            // The material search will happen when TryUpgradeToTranslucentImage is called
            return null;
        }
```


</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Good question. TranslucentImage requires a few things to work:

1. **TranslucentImageSource** - A component that captures and blurs what's behind the UI. The game likely has this set up somewhere (on a camera or root UI element).

2. **Proper material** - We tried creating one from the shader, but it might need specific settings.

3. **The blur region** - TranslucentImage blurs what's *behind* it in the scene, not just tinting.

When we tried it before, we created the material but the blur wasn't showing. The issue might be that our canvas isn't connected to the game's TranslucentImageSource.

Could you use the UIAssetInspector to check a few things on the IC10 editor's TranslucentImage:
1. What's the **material** property showing? (the actual material instance)
2. Is there a **TranslucentImageSource** component anywhere in the IC10 window hierarchy or on a parent canvas?

If we can find the game's TranslucentImageSource or the exact material it uses, we might be able to reference it directly instead of creating our own.

Alternatively, the game might have a global TranslucentImageSource that all TranslucentImages automatically connect to - we just need to make sure our canvas is set up correctly to use it.

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

This is incredibly helpful! Now I understand the IC10 structure clearly:

**IC10 Editor Structure:**
1. **PanelInputCode** - `(no sprite)` with `#000000BF` (black 75%) - this is the darkening overlay behind EVERYTHING
2. **Image (window-shadow)** - 42px border, `#FFFFFF80` (white 50%) - outer shadow
3. **Image (dialog-bg)** - **TranslucentImage** with `#002A47FF`, inset 12px from window-shadow - this creates the blur effect
4. **Outline (dialog-outline)** - `#FF6516FF` orange, inset 10px from window-shadow

The key insight: The **darker area inside the code editor** comes from:
- The `Mask`'s grandparent has `button-bg color:#00000068` 
- That's a separate element inside the dialog that creates the darker code editing area

So there are TWO levels:
1. The outer window frame (dialog-bg with TranslucentImage showing lighter blue through blur)
2. The inner code area (button-bg with #00000068 - black at 41%) which makes it darker

The "lighter blue" portion is the TranslucentImage dialog-bg showing through, and the "darker" portion where the code lines are has an additional darkening layer.

For our Station Planner, since we can't use TranslucentImage properly, we should:
1. Use the lighter blue color for the outer frame (dialog-bg)
2. Use a darker color for the inner editing area

Let me update the code to reflect this properly - the OUTER editor panel should be lighter (#002A47), and the INNER content area should be darker.

The user wants to change the outline color to #000f1f. Let me convert that hex to RGB values:
- 00 = 0/255 = 0
- 0f = 15/255 = 0.059
- 1f = 31/255 = 0.122

So the color should be new Color(0f, 0.059f, 0.122f, 1f)

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

Looking at the data carefully:

When over the **dark input area**, we see:
- **Grandparent: Mask** with `GP Image: button-bg color:#00000068`

This is the key! The **Mask** element (grandparent of the code lines) has `button-bg` sprite with color `#00000068` (black at ~41% opacity). This is what creates the darker inner area where the code is!

When over the **lighter window area**, we don't see this Mask element - we only see the dialog-bg with TranslucentImage.

So the structure is:
1. **PanelInputCode** - `#000000BF` (black 75%) - outermost darkening
2. **window-shadow** - shadow sprite
3. **dialog-bg (TranslucentImage)** - `#002A47FF` - the lighter blue area with blur
4. **dialog-outline** - orange border
5. **ScrollRect/Mask** - `button-bg #00000068` - THIS is the dark inner content area!

The Mask element that contains the scroll content has `button-bg` sprite with `#00000068` color - this creates the darker rectangle where the code goes!

Our tool IS showing this info in the first dump:
```
  Parent: Content
    Grandparent: Mask
    GP Image: button-bg color:#00000068
```

So the dark border around the code area comes from the **Mask** element using `button-bg` sprite with `#00000068` color. The "border" effect we see is probably the contrast between the lighter TranslucentImage area and this darker masked content area.

We're already showing the grandparent image info, so the tool isn't missing anything. The user just needs to understand the layering.

For our Station Planner, the dark area inside our panels should use `button-bg #00000068` on the viewport/mask area. Let me check if that's what we're doing... we had set it to `#000000BF` (75% black) but IC10 uses `#00000068` (41% black).
 
I notice the visual border emerges from strategic layering. The dialog-bg sits 12px inside the window-shadow, while the Mask area is further inset. This precise positioning creates a subtle lighter rim around the dark content area, generating a soft visual boundary between different interface elements.

The user is right. The IC10 creates the border effect by having a GAP between two layers:
1. Outer: dialog-bg with #002A47FF (lighter blue) - this shows as the "border"
2. Inner: Mask/viewport with button-bg #00000068 (darker) - inset further in

So the "border" isn't a separate element - it's just the visible edge of the lighter background showing around the darker inner area.

1. Have the outer background (dialog-bg) at #002A47FF
2. Have the inner content/viewport inset MORE so there's a visible gap of the lighter blue showing around it

- dialog-bg is inset 12px from window-shadow
- The Mask (content area) would be further inset from dialog-bg

```
            inputOutlineRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            inputOutlineRect.offsetMin = Vector2.zero;
            inputOutlineRect.offsetMax = Vector2.zero;
            var inputOutlineImg = inputOutlineGO.AddComponent<Image>();
            var inputOutlineSprite = LoadSlicedSprite("slot-outline.png", 7);
            if (inputOutlineSprite != null)
            {
                inputOutlineImg.sprite = inputOutlineSprite;
                inputOutlineImg.type = Image.Type.Sliced;
            }
            inputOutlineImg.color = new Color(0f, 0f, 0f, .85f); // #000a1a - darker navy
            inputOutlineImg.raycastTarget = false;
            
            Debug.Log("[Station Planner] Editor panel created with IC10-style TMP_InputField");
        }]]
```

- The inner content (Mask with button-bg) is `#00000068` (41% black overlay)

The user is frustrated that my attempts aren't matching the IC10 editor's appearance. Let me think about this more carefully.

Looking at the IC10 screenshot they shared earlier - it has:
1. A dark interior where the code is displayed
2. A very clear, crisp dark border around it
3. The border looks almost black/very dark navy

The IC10 structure from the UIAssetInspector was:
- PanelInputCode: #000000BF (black 75%), no sprite
- window-shadow: 42px borders, #FFFFFF80
- dialog-bg (TranslucentImage): #002A47FF, inset 12px
- dialog-outline: 18px, orange #FF6516FF
- Mask: button-bg with #00000068

Wait - the "dialog-outline" in IC10 is ORANGE (#FF6516FF). That's the actual active selection outline, not the dark border effect.

The dark border effect must come from something else. Let me think...

The PanelInputCode has #000000BF (75% black) - this is the outermost darkening layer.

Then there's window-shadow with #FFFFFF80 (50% white) - this creates a subtle glow/shadow effect.

Then dialog-bg with TranslucentImage at #002A47FF - this is the blurred background that creates the nice effect.

The "border" effect they're seeing is likely the combination of:
1. The outer dark PanelInputCode 
2. The window-shadow creating some brightness
3. The dialog-bg with translucent blur
4. Then the Mask with button-bg creating the darker interior

The contrast between the TranslucentImage blur (which picks up colors from behind) and the dark Mask creates the border appearance.

Since we can't get TranslucentImage working, maybe I should try to manually create this layered effect:
1. Outer dark layer (like PanelInputCode)
2. 

I'll experiment with adding a slightly brighter border layer to create depth and visual separation. The color discrepancy might stem from incorrect color interpretation or unexpected rendering overrides.

```
            _fileTreeScrollRect.horizontal = false;
            _fileTreeScrollRect.vertical = true;

            _fileTreeScrollRect.scrollSensitivity = 25f;
            
            // Border using dialog-outline sprite for rounded corners
            var borderGO = new GameObject("Border");
            borderGO.transform.SetParent(panel.transform, false);
            var borderRect = borderGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            borderRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            borderRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
            borderRect.offsetMin = new Vector2(4, 4);
            borderRect.offsetMax = new Vector2(-4, -4);

            vpRect.anchorMin = Vector2.zero;
            vpRect.anchorMax = Vector2.one;
```

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

This is really helpful! Now I can see the IC10 structure clearly:

**IC10 Editor Inner Area Structure:**
1. `PanelInputCode` - `#000000BF` (no sprite) - outer darkening
2. `Image` (window-shadow) - `#FFFFFF80` - the shadow layer
3. `Image` (dialog-bg) - `#002A47FF` with **TranslucentImage** material - this is the lighter blue with blur effect
4. `Outline` (dialog-outline) - `#FF6516FF` - orange outline (inset 10px, so 2px gap from dialog-bg which is at 12px)
5. `Mask` (button-bg) - `#00000068` - the dark content area

The key insight I missed: The **border effect** isn't from a gap - it's from the `dialog-outline` sprite which is a dedicated outline sprite! And it's using `dialog-outline.png` not `slot-outline.png`.

Looking at the structure:
- `dialog-bg` at offset 12px 
- `Outline` (dialog-outline) at offset 10px - so it renders OUTSIDE the dialog-bg slightly (2px visible border)

Wait no, let me re-read:
- `Image` (dialog-bg): `offsetMin: (12.00, 12.00) offsetMax: (-12.00, -12.00)` - inset 12px
- `Outline` (dialog-outline): `offsetMin: (10.00, 10.00) offsetMax: (-10.00, -10.00)` - inset 10px

So the outline is LARGER than the dialog-bg (less inset), meaning the orange outline shows around the edge of the dialog-bg.

But the user's screenshot shows a dark border, not orange. Let me look at the dark area specifically...

Looking at `[5] Image` (the dark content area):


The Mask with button-bg at #00000068 creates a subtle dark boundary. The visual separation comes from the slight color and opacity difference between the translucent dialog background and the darker content area mask, creating a natural border effect without an explicit outline.

I realize the green border looks square because the current Image lacks a rounded sprite. By switching to `dialog-outline.png` with rounded corners, I can achieve the desired soft-edged appearance. The color variations across different UI elements like the Editor, PanelInputCode, and Border reveal the nuanced layering of translucency and color.

The TranslucentImage's vibrancy and brightness settings are crucial, generating that distinctive lighter blue visual effect that distinguishes the background from surrounding elements. Without these precise settings, the background would appear flat and uniform.

Examining IC10's design reveals a sophisticated approach: using a dark `Mask` with `button-bg` sprite and a strategic color overlay creates depth through the gap between translucent layers.

I'll experiment with `dialog-bg.png`, which offers 18px rounded corners, to craft a more refined border element that mimics the desired visual hierarchy.

_**Agent (copilot/claude-opus-4.5)**_

The user is frustrated. Let me re-read the IC10 inspector output carefully:

From the IC10 editor dark part:
```
[5] Image
  Path: PanelInputCode/Image/Image
  RectTransform:
    sizeDelta: (-24.00, -24.00)
    anchorMin: (0.00, 0.00) anchorMax: (1.00, 1.00)
    pivot: (0.50, 0.50)
    offsetMin: (12.00, 12.00) offsetMax: (-12.00, -12.00)
  Image: dialog-bg
    Texture: dialog-bg (64x64)
    TexFormat: DXT5, isReadable: False
    Border(9-slice): L=18 B=18 R=18 T=18
    SpriteRect: (x:0.00, y:0.00, width:64.00, height:64.00)
    Color: #002A47FF
    Type: Sliced, RaycastTarget: True
    Material: Default-Translucent
    maskable: True, preserveAspect: False
    == TranslucentImage ==
    vibrancy: 2.000
    brightness: -0.257
    flatten: 0.200
    spriteBlending: 1.000
    TI.material: Default-Translucent
    TI.shader: UI/TranslucentImage
    TI.materialForRendering: Default-Translucent
```

So the TranslucentImage is using:
- `dialog-bg` sprite (which HAS rounded corners via 9-slice!)
- Material: Default-Translucent
- The TranslucentImage component uses the sprite's shape for masking

The user is asking how TranslucentImage can have rounded corners. The answer is: **TranslucentImage uses the sprite as a mask!** The `dialog-bg.png` sprite has rounded corners via its 9-slice setup, and the TranslucentImage effect is applied WITHIN that sprite's shape.
 
The rounded effect emerges from a clever layering technique. The 9-slice `dialog-bg` sprite provides a precise 18-pixel border mask, while the TranslucentImage component applies a blurred, vibrant effect within those boundaries. The visual contrast comes from the bright, slightly darkened blur against a semi-transparent background, creating a sophisticated, glowing appearance.

The "border" isn't a traditional outline, but a strategic visual transition between two masked layers: a bright translucent background and a darker inner mask. This technique allows for seamless rounded corners with a subtle, sophisticated visual depth.

To recreate this effect without TranslucentImage, I'll focus on achieving a deep, nearly black border that mimics the original's visual complexity. The key is capturing the nuanced darkness while maintaining the design's elegant rounded edge approach.
